Over the millennium that they mined the rich mineral deposits of the Shattered Range, the [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) of Dongun Hold discovered the stalwart mjolgat, a beast that exhibits the uncanny ability to sniff out ore deposits with its incredible sense of smell. To this day, dwarves across Golarion rear mjolgats as companions and draft animals, training them to locate precious metals hidden deep beneath the surface. Some dauntless dwarves even turn mjolgats into mounts, though those brave enough to do so often require ample ear protection, as the creatures release horrifying shrieks when threatened. Exhibiting a temperament that rivals even a dwarf's surliness, mjolgats have large, cranial crests of bone as broad and heavy as the head of a giant's warhammer. While the rest of their face resembles a cross between an elephant and a yak—with elongated snouts, rodentlike muzzles, and a two sets of horns—their bodies resemble those of squat boars. A straggly mane of thick brown fur covers their massive necks, which develop and strengthen over their life spans to support the massive columns of bone atop their heads. Adolescent mjolgats often lack such athleticism, walking backward and dragging their heads behind them until they can hold their necks upright. A dwarf down on their luck is often compared to a young mjolgat unable to lift their head off the ground. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Mjolgat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3302) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 175 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), orescent ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -2 **Orescent** A mjolgat can detect the ores of unrefined precious metals with its powerful nose. Common earth and stone do not impede the mjolgat from smelling ores deep within the ground. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +8 **Will** +8 **HP** 60 **Head On** **Trigger** A creature the mjolgat can see targets the mjolgat with an attack; **Effect** The mjolgat swings its crest in the direction of the danger, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** bone crest +10, **Damage** 2d10+7 bludgeoning **Melee** hoof +12, **Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning **Hammerhead** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The mjolgat rears upon its hind leg to crush an enemy with its jagged crest of bone. The mjolgat makes a bone crest Strike; on a hit, the mjolgat deals an extra die of damage. This counts as two attacks when calculating the mjolgat's multiple attack penalty. **Punch-Drunk** If the mjolgat critically fails a bone crest Strike, it becomes [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 1 round. **Rockbreaker** When the mjolgat deals damage to an object (such as a shield or an ore deposit), it deals double damage. **Shrieking Slam** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The mjolgat lets out a terrifying screech before bashing its head into the ground. Creatures within 30 feet of the mjolgat must attempt a DC 21 Will save. Regardless of the result of the save, they are then immune to Shrieking Slam.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76).
**Failure** The target is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The target is frightened 3 and [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round.
name: Mjolgat

It's also immune to penalties to its Speeds and the immobilized condition, and it ignores difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) Huge, 3d10+9 bludgeoning, Rupture 30 **Thrash** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The rumindrol attempts individual Strikes against each creature in its reach. The large scavenger birds known as scroungefeathers make their homes near the sites of large battles. These birds feast on blood and carrion and have quickly learned that battlefields provide both in abundance. In spite of this grisly diet, they're best known for another strange adaptation: they build their nests from pieces of armor, weapons, and magic items scavenged on the battlefields. This leaves young scroungefeathers remarkably well protected, allowing both parents to leave the nest to search for food. Alarmingly, scroungefeathers have learned how to use the items they scavenge for their nests to fend off attackers, flinging arrowheads, thrashing about with knives, and even activating the occasional wand. A scroungefeather nest makes a tempting target for adventurers and thieves, as the treasures tucked within can be quite valuable, but the birds defend their nests fiercely, particularly when there are eggs or chicks inside. While the feathers of scroungefeathers don't have any magical properties, they're popular among thieves. Insignia for thieves' guilds often include the image of a black feather with two crimson stripes, and such organizations often send new recruits to raid a scroungefeather nest as part of their training. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Scroungefeather](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3304) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 177 **Perception** +13; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +14 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** -4 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +16 **Will** +8 **HP** 76 --- **Speed** 20 feet; fly 40 feet **Melee** beak +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d8+3 piercing **Melee** talon +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d6+3 slashing **Grab Debris** **Requirements** The scroungefeather is in its nest or another environment rich with debris; **Effect** The scroungefeather uses an Interact action to grab an item with its beak, selected randomly; roll 1d4 to determine the type of debris. The scroungefeather gains access to the listed abilities until it uses Tossed Scraps to discard the debris or until it Releases the debris. The scroungefeather can't use its beak attack while it is using its beak to Grab Debris.
**d4****Item** **Effect**
1 Armor scrap The scroungefeather gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC.
2 Shattered blade The scroungefeather gains a broken blade melee Strike with a +12 attack modifier that deals 2d10+6 slashing damage.
3 Unexploded bomb When the scroungefeather throws this with Tossed Scraps, all the damage is fire damage, and the bomb also deals 3 fire [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage.
4 Faulty wand The scroungefeather gains a magic bolt ranged Strike with a +14 attack modifier that deals 2d6+6 force damage.
**Junk Nest** The scroungefeather's nest of sharp metallic junk covers a 15-foot-by-15-foot area. The area is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454) to any non-scroungefeather creatures. A creature that moves on the ground through the nest takes 3 piercing damage for each square of the area it moves into. Scroungefeathers can [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) at any point in the nest. **Tossed Scraps** **Requirements** The scroungefeather has Grabbed Debris; **Effect** The scroungefeather flings the debris at a target within 20 feet, making an attack roll with a +12 modifier. On a success, the target takes 2d10+5 bludgeoning damage.
name: Scroungefeather

Harbor seals are the most common seals found in Golarion and famous for their soft, furry bodies. Enemies adjacent to the leopard seal when it ends its movement take 2d10 bludgeoning damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) and are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) on a failure.

These reptilian beasts of burden are primarily known for their telepathic nature and close bond with their riders. Shotalashus are native to the planet Castrovel, where they are the traditional mounts of lashunta warriors, though some have appeared on Golarion thanks to travel between the two worlds. Both [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2) and lashuntas have brought shotalashus to Golarion, though elves are somewhat more likely to sell or give them away. Mounted combatants covet these alien creatures, though taming one is not as simple as buying a horse or camel. A shotalashu must form a telepathic bond with their rider before any riding is permitted, and once the creature forms such a bond, they will not permit any other rider. While telepaths have the easiest time forming this bond, others have found ways to prove their worth. Any shotalashus encountered in the wilds of Golarion most likely escaped from captivity—a notable wild pack exists on the Isle of Kortos, descended from such runaways. Would-be riders are most likely to find available shotalashus in Absalom, Kyonin, and southern Garund. Shotalashus are extravagantly expensive to raise on Golarion, resulting in corresponding prices when they appear on the market. However, the friends of riders who've passed will often help the surviving shotalashus find a new rider once they're ready. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Shotalashu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3309) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 180 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Lashunta](/Languages.aspx?ID=177); (can't speak any language); empathic communication **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +7 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Empathic Communication** While a shotalashu can't speak, even telepathically, it understands simple commands in the languages it knows. It returns telepathic sensations of emotion to creatures touching it or the partner of its telepathic link at any distance.
**Telepathic Link** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) A rider who would tame a shotalashu for a mount must first form a telepathic link with it. Establishing this link require spending a week with the desired mount and succeeding at a DC 21 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check. Any creature with telepathic capabilities, such as [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79), touch telepathy, empathic sense, or the ability to cast spells such as [_telepathy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719), gains a +4 circumstance bonus to this check. This link remains and neither can form a new link until one member dies. The shock of a partner dying leaves the survivor [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for 24 hours and prevents forming a new link for at least a month. --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +10 **Will** +11 **HP** 35 --- **Speed** 40 feet, jungle stride **Melee** claws +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6+2 slashing plus 1d4 mental **Jungle Stride** The shotalashu ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) due to vegetation. **Telepathic Pounce** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The shotalashu hunts by pinning its foes with its mind. One creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 18 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a –5 foot status penalty to its Speeds for one round.
**Failure** The creature takes a –10 foot status penalty to its Speeds for one round. The shotalashu can then [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298).
**Critical Failure** The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for one round. The shotalashu can Leap.
name: Shotalashu

<title level="1" pfs="">[Sky Fisher](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3310)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The sky fisher is an unusual aerial predator, mostly found in tropical jungle regions but occasionally seen as far north as Varisia. Naturalists believe it's a distant relative of the jellyfish that has evolved into a new ecological niche through prolonged exposure to elemental energies from the [Plane of Air](/Planes.aspx?ID=29). That the biggest, most aggressive sky fishers are found (and thought to have originated) near powerful loci of elemental air, such as in Garund's Terwa Uplands or around the Eye of Abendego, lends further credence to this theory.

 In appearance, a sky fisher resembles an enormous jellyfish, its many dangling tendrils hanging beneath a clear bulbous body. Although its appearance is quite difficult to accurately determine, as its natural transparent form is almost completely invisible, its inside contain a complex chemical factory that produces lighter-than-air gases, paralyzing toxins, and flesh-eating enzymes. These enzymes are highly potent and often desired by toxicologists due to their unique properties. The creature hunts by hovering just above the forest canopy, using eye-like clusters on the ends of its tentacles as a kind of inverted periscope. When it spots prey, it floats close and waits for an opportune moment to sting. Then, as paralysis takes hold, the sky fisher hoists its meal into the sky to slowly consume it. Inside, enzymes break down the dead or paralyzed victims into a bloody slurry.

 A recently fed sky fisher can always be quickly identified by the looping coils of blood that circulate through its body—a beautiful, if terrible, sight.

Using non-[hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) does not cause the sky fisher to become [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79). **Abduct** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) The sky fisher reels in a target grabbed by its tentacles, pulling them into an adjacent space, and then attempts to [Swallow them Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) (Large, 3d8+12 acid, Rupture 25). The sky fisher can only use Swallow Whole when using Abduct.

**Enzymic Vent** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The sky fisher vents flesh-eating enzymes into the air, dealing 3d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) and 3d6 persistent bleed damage in a 20-foot emanation (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). It can't use Enzymic Vent again for 1d4 rounds.

**Jet** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The sky fisher quickly expels some of its gases to move swiftly through the air, [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to 100 feet in a straight line; this movement doesn't trigger reactions.

**Sky Fisher Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 3d6 poison damage, [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), and can't speak above a whisper (1 round); **Stage 2** 3d8 poison damage, clumsy 2, and can't speak (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d10 poison damage and [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) (1 round)

Guardian guides take any size or shape, from quiet mouse spirits that protect families from pestilence and locusts to mighty sunscale serpents who embody the watchful wrath of the sun itself. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 34 **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 34 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Sunscale Serpent](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3311) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 182 **Perception** +25; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 100 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +33, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +6 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +26 **Will** +28 **HP** 251 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision **Resistances** all damage 14 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)) **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** tail only --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** jaws +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+11 force plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Melee** tail +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+11 force plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 31 - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Bond with Mortal** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The spirit guide spends 10 minutes to form a bond with a mortal creature. While the bond exists, the spirit guide increases their current and maximum Hit Points by 28, gains a +2 status bonus to their attack and damage rolls, and can communicate telepathically with the bonded mortal as long as the two beings are on the same plane. The spirit guide can only be bonded with one mortal at a time, and they can take this action again to end the bond or to form a new bond (which also ends the old bond). The bond also ends if the spirit guide or the mortal dies.
This bond strengthens the spirit guide's connection to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). While bonded, the spirit guide loses the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) and [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) traits, loses their immunity to disease, paralysis, and poison, along with their resistance to all damage, and changes their Strikes to deal the appropriate amount of physical damage (typically piercing or slashing) instead of force damage. **Bonded Strike** **Requirements** The sunscale serpent is currently Bonded with a Mortal; **Effect** The sunscale serpent makes a jaws Strike. If this attack hits, the bonded mortal can spend their reaction to Strike the same target. **Sun's Heat** The sunscale serpent [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to its fly Speed. All creatures directly below the spaces it moves through must succeed at a DC 31 Fortitude save or be exposed to sun's touch poison. The serpent cannot fly further than 60 feet above the target or the poison becomes too dispersed in the fall to take effect. **Sun's Touch** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 34; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 6d8 poison damage and [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) (1 round); **Stage 2** 8d6 poison damage and clumsy 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** 6d10 poison damage and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) (1 round) **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** Large, 2d10+9 force and 2d10 fire, Rupture 32 **Unleash the Sun** **Requirement** The sunscale serpent is flying; **Effect** The sunscale serpent Flies up to its fly Speed, then crashes to the ground, releasing a wave of heat dealing 5d10 fire damage to all creatures within a 60-foot burst and searing their eyes with the erupting glory of its scales. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 31 Reflex save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes full damage, is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round, and dazzled for 1 minute.
name: Sunscale Serpent

Swift guides are often among the first spirit guides to respond to threats, and they're said to move with such incredible speed that it's impossible to tell whether they're one or many until the battle is resolved. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 23 **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Mirror Wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3312) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 183 **Perception** +18; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +17 **Str** +3 **Dex** +6 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -2 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +19 **Will** +15 **HP** 117 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision **Resistances** all damage 7 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-magical) **Visage Strike** **Trigger** A creature adjacent to the mirror wolf's visages damages mirror wolf's ally; **Effect** The mirror wolf teleports to the visage's spot, destroying the visage, and makes a jaws Strike. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** jaws +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+8 force plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 22 - **3rd** [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Bond with Mortal** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The spirit guide spends 10 minutes to form a bond with a mortal creature. While the bond exists, the spirit guide increases their current and maximum Hit Points by 14, gains a +2 status bonus to their attack and damage rolls, and can communicate telepathically with the bonded mortal as long as the two beings are on the same plane. The spirit guide can only be bonded with one mortal at a time, and they can take this action again to end the bond or to form a new bond (which also ends the old bond). The bond also ends if the spirit guide or the mortal dies.
This bond strengthens the spirit guide's connection to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). While bonded, the spirit guide loses the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) and [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) traits, loses their immunity to disease, paralysis, and poison, along with their resistance to all damage, and changes their Strikes to deal the appropriate amount of physical damage (typically piercing or slashing) instead of force damage. **Strafing Strike** The mirror wolf makes a jaws Strike against a creature within range. The mirror wolf can then Stride and make a second jaws Strike against the same creature. **Bonded Strike** **Requirements** The mirror wolf is currently Bonded with a Mortal; **Effect** The mirror wolf makes a jaws Strike. If this attack hits, the bonded mortal can spend their reaction to Strike the same target. **Lingering Assailant** ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The mirror wolf attacks with such speed it leaves a visage of itself behind. When the mirror wolf Strikes, they leave behind a visage in an adjacent square. The visage is treated as an ally for effects such as flanking and pack attack. A visage has AC 10 and 1 HP and lasts for 1 round. **Pack Attack** The mirror wolf's Strikes deal 1d8 extra damage to creatures within reach of at least two of the mirror wolf's allies.
name: Mirror Wolf

<title level="1" pfs="">[Stony Goat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3313)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Stony goats diverge from their common cousins via their rock-like horns and unique appetites. These meek creatures love to eat mineral ores, using teeth tougher than diamonds to grind down rocks and metals. The indigestible components, such as refined chunks of metal and precious stones, coagulate into a cud the goat regurgitates to further ruminate on. In emergency situations, stony goats can even drain nutrition from their cud. These properties might make stony goats excellent companions for miners and metalworkers; however, the beasts are quite rare and famously difficult to train.

 Stony goats are jumpy creatures with a prey animal's wide field of view and a strong survival instinct. They also have a unique defense mechanism: when startled, stony goats temporarily petrify into solid stone, dissuading predators through inedibility. This combination of weak nerves and tremendous climbing ability often leads to travelers discovering what appears to be a lone statue of a goat in an otherwise unoccupied mountainous area.

 Keen observers can discern a stony goat's recent diet via the mineral flakes that form in the goat's horns, such as light silver streaks indicating a meal of dawnsilver ore. Collectors often hunt stony goats and display horns with rare properties. If a stony goat destroys its cud for emergency nutrition, its horns appear as solid chunks of the minerals it consumed for about an hour before reverting to their rock-like norm.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Storm Snake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3314)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Storm snakes are reclusive creatures only seen in the wild during intense storms. The metallic scales that cover their bodies attract and absorb nearby lightning strikes, suffusing the creatures with a halo of blue or white light. Because storm snakes are most often sighted in solitude and disappear without a trace as soon as a storm recedes, a lot of folklore across Golarion considers them to be the emissaries of storm deities and harbingers of disaster.

 Before live clutches of storm snake young were first discovered, it was hypothesized that the creatures were made of mist and vapor; many assumed them to be illusory shapes within the clouds themselves or fleeting dragon sightings. New zoological research has found, however, that storm snakes are actually a distant cousin of dragons. Raised on mountaintops in small broods, storm snakes have underdeveloped limbs and lack any form of wing. Instead, they expand and contract their metallic scales to capture the static electricity in wind currents, which keeps them aloft during high winds and storms.

 Research has also shed light on the diet of these naturally shy creatures. Rather than consuming electricity, as many who'd observed them in storms believed, storm snakes are herbivorous, enjoying most varieties of grasses, plants, or tree leaves. This diet, considered alongside their flight patterns during thunderstorms, has been theorized to be a form of symbiosis; after all, any lightning strikes absorbed by a storm snake during a storm can't damage the plant growth below. Additionally, safely discharged electricity from a storm snake has been observed to stimulate growth in a wide variety of flora, all while having no adverse affects on the plants' palatability or nutritional benefits. Many farmers have tried in vain to capture, tame, or otherwise attract storm snakes to their fields to invigorate their crops during tough times, though few have succeeded.

On a failure, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn.

**Static Field** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The storm snake gathers all static electricity in the area before releasing it in a 30-foot emanation that deals 3d12 electricity damage to all non-[plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) creatures (DC 19 Reflex save) and grants plant creatures 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute. Plant life in the area begins to grow significantly faster than the average for plants of their genus; in forests, fields, or otherwise floral locations, this immediately transforms the area into nonmagical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). The storm snake can't use Static field again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Taldan Cave Squirrel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3315)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Taldan cave squirrels, also known as tauhotis, are a curious cousin of the much smaller common ground squirrel, found anywhere with large, grassy flatlands. The creatures burrow underground, forming cave systems tall enough for giants in astonishingly little time. Once the caves are excavated, the squirrels turn their attention toward the largest quantities of gems or metals they can find—including those in the ground floors and vaults of nearby buildings. Nothing is off-limits, and no effort is too large when these squirrels go gem hunting.

 Cave squirrels' desire for these precious stones and ores arises not from greed, but rather productivity. After returning to the cave system, cheek pouches full of treasure, the males deposit the stolen goods before the females, who lick and spit on the piles of gems and metals to form them into structural pillars. Given the cave squirrels' tireless work habits, these pillars can solidify in a matter of hours, easily displacing existing stone and soil. Many an Opparan groundskeeper has stepped outside after a lovely teatime to find months of work undone in a scene of utter, glittering chaos. Architects throughout history have attempted to recreate or imitate these gleaming pillars; however, they rarely succeeded, for they often lacked a certain unpredictable pragmatism the cave squirrels seem to effortlessly achieve.

 When threatened, cave squirrels will let out a high-pitched scream to warn their colony. They can spit out any food or building materials in their cheek pouches with deadly speeds at attackers.

The cave squirrel can't use Screaming Force again for 1d4 rounds.

**Tun State** <actions string="Free Action" /> **Trigger** The tardigrade would be reduced to 0 Hit Points or would die due to [starvation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2603), [suffocation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2436), or similar environmental causes; **Frequency** once per week; **Requirements** The tardigrade isn't already in tun state; **Effect** The giant tardigrade doesn't die but instead remains at 1 Hit Point and curls into a dry ball, called a tun. While in this tun state, the giant tardigrade is [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), doesn't need to eat, drink, or breathe, and doubles its resistance to 20. It remains in tun state indefinitely until covered in significant amounts of water, at which point it begins to rehydrate, gaining [regeneration 5](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=72). All creatures in a 20-foot cone must succeed at a DC 25 Reflex save or be pulled adjacent to the giant tardigrade. The tardigrade chooses one creature that failed its save, Grabbing the target in its claws and making a stylet Strike.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Karkadann](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3318)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Karkadanns are a distant relation to unicorns, favoring more open terrain than their woodland kin. With the build of a buffalo, karkadanns have gray, scaly bodies, thicker hooves, and no mane. Despite their territoriality and harsh temperament, many karkadanns can be befriended by those who truly hold respect for nature in their hearts.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Alicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3320)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Alicorns are a creation of arcane intervention, a rare transformation that occurs when a unicorn is exposed to large sources of magic. Massive in size, their strong bodies are matched with two large wings on their back, a long spiraling horn, and a mane that continually ripples in an unseen wind.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 39

**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 39

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Alicorn.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 15">[Alicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3320)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Huge" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=599" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 191

**Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +27, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +30, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +8

**Dex** +7

**Con** +4

**Int** +6

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Wild Empathy** The alicorn has an empathic connection to the creatures of the natural world that. The alicorn can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531) and to make very simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) of them.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 36 

**Fort** +26 

**Ref** +26 

**Will** +29 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 320

</row>

**Immunities**
[poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)

**Draw in Magic** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of the alicorn Casts a Spell; **Effect** The alicorn attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell (counteract modifier +28, counteract rank 8th). If successful, the alicorn can choose to gain the effects of the triggering spell as its sole target.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, fly 80 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn +29 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 4d10+10 piercing plus 2d6 spirit and ghost touch

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +29 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 4d6+10 piercing plus 2d6 spirit and ghost touch

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
wing +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 4d8+10 bludgeoning and ghost touch

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 36
- **Cantrips (8th)**
[Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585)
- **1st**
[Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542) (at will)
- **5th**
[Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448)
- **6th**
[Telepathy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719)
- **8th**
[Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749)

**Aerial Attack** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The alicorn [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) and Strikes twice with their wings at any points during their movement. Each attack counts toward the alicorn's multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn't increase until after they make all the attacks.

**Beam of Light** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) The alicorn harnesses a powerful burning ray of light from their horn. Creatures in a 120-foot line take 16d6 fire damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). An [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creature in the area takes an additional 8d6 spirit damage. If the light passes through an area of magical [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569) or targets a creature affected by magical darkness, the beam attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the darkness (counteract modifier +28, counteract rank 8th). The alicorn can't use its Beam of Light again for 1d4 rounds.

**Ghost Touch**   An alicorn's Strikes have the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune.

</column>

name: Alicorn

markdown:
These tenacious little rodents travel in massive swarms across the desert, using the sun both as shield and sword during their long marches. Unlike other rodents, vibrant pups are eusocial, causing them to behave more like insects than other rodents. Only the queen can reproduce, and is fiercely protected for that reason. This social structure enables them to viciously attack and act as a single unit. Each of the pups also have specific roles within the family and the swarm. The worker pups gather and dig while in a den, but will act as a shield to protect the more valuable fighter pups while swarming. Vibrant pups spend most of their lives underground, digging out elaborate tunnels in search for food. However, in the harsh deserts of Thuvia, food can be scarce. A family of vibrant pups will abandon their dens and set out on harsh multiday journeys in search of land that can sustain the queen long enough to have another litter of pups. These journeys can happen up to three times a year, following seasonal changes in resources. Outside of these local migrations, swarms will leave their dens only to repel a massive threat to the queen. Vibrant pups have unusual and beautiful anatomical features. Their skin, while soft, is extremely reflective, which serves a dual purpose for the rodents since it can both reflect the harsh sun away to better thermoregulate as well as focus those rays toward threats, especially when multiple pups work in unison. The glinting of a pup swarm in migration can be seen from afar—rather than inviting challenges, this obvious display signals would-be predators to steer clear. As of late, their reflective, soft skin has become quite fashionable in wealthy circles. While hunting such small creatures for their pelts is extremely impractical and dangerous, the high price tag has led to more frequent hunts. While these rodents do serve a unique ecological niche, many farmers are more than happy to rid their fields of these voracious eaters and will often point out nearby hives to local hunters. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Vibrant Pup Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3321) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 192 **Perception** +22; [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411)) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16 **Str** -2 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** -4 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +21 **Will** +18 **HP** 140 **Immunities** precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78) **Resistances** bludgeoning 5, piercing 10, slashing 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 5, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage 5 **Reflective Skin** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 10 feet. Other creatures in the aura are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65). The aura automatically activates when the swarm is in [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407) and deactivates in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409).
**Feel No Pain** **Trigger** The vibrant pup swarm is critically hit by a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306); **Effect** The worker pups become insensitive to pain and leap to the front. The swarm gains 10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 round. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 30 **Swarming Bites** Each enemy in the swarm's space takes 2d6 piercing damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). **Focused Reflection** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) **Requirements** The vibrant pup swarm is in [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407); **Effect** The swarm simultaneously turns to focus the light in an arcing beam, dealing 6d12 fire damage to all creatures in a 30-foot cone (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). A creature that fails its save is also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round (or 1 minute on a critical failure). The swarm can't use Focused Reflection again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Vibrant Pup Swarm

markdown:
Virtuosic lyrebirds present an excellent case study in how specialty-bred animals can escape captivity and become pests. A small team of apprentice mages established the original virtuosic lyrebirds as part of their graduation thesis, in an attempt to create a familiar that could better aid in spellcasting. The vocal mimicry of these birds did indeed allow them to harmonize with their bonded spellcaster; however, initial progress was slow, and the impatient students chose to hastily elevate the birds' abilities before their thesis deadline using experimental magic. Unfortunately, the specimens' newfound vocal mimicry was so supreme that they could mimic spell incantations as easily as any other sound they heard. The virtuosic lyrebirds soon vaporized, teleported out of, or befuddled their keepers into opening their cages, escaping into the wild and flourishing. Virtuosic lyrebirds use their spellcasting abilities mostly to play pranks and alleviate boredom. They tend to travel in pairs, but when these pairs congregate into larger flocks, their mischief and fun can quickly turn dangerous. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Virtuosic Lyrebird](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3322) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 193 **Perception** +14 **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +14, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +17 **Will** +14 **HP** 65 **Counter-Melody** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), magic) **Trigger** An opponent within 60 feet Casts a Spell; **Effect** The virtuosic lyrebird makes a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against the triggering spell (+14 counteract modifier). --- **Speed** 15 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** beak +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d8+4 piercing **Melee** talon +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d6+4 piercing **Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Bullhorn](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) - **2nd** [Breathe Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457), [Shatter](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670) - **3rd** [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Hypnotize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564) **Arcane Harmony** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) **Effect** The virtuosic lyrebird sings a subtle trill that others can use to embellish their magic. The next virtuosic lyrebird to Cast a Spell within 60 feet of the first lyrebird that used Arcane Harmony chooses one of the following two benefits, after which the Arcane Harmony ends.
  • The spell gains a +2 status bonus to damage per spell rank.
  • One target of the spell takes a –2 status penalty to saves against the spell.
**Spell Mimicry** **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** A creature Casts a Spell of 3rd rank or lower since the end of the virtuosic lyrebird' s last turn, and the virtuosic lyrebird heard the incantation for the spell; **Effect** The virtuosic lyrebird learns the spell enough to mimic and cast it this turn, using as many actions as the original spell took. **Spellsong Casting** The virtuosic lyrebird's exquisite vocal control lets it cast spells purely through its song. While it must be able to vocalize to Cast a Spell, it doesn't need to gesture, removing the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait from any spell that it casts. If it Casts a Spell with the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) trait (usually with its Spell Mimicry), it can Cast that Spell in [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), even though the bird doesn't truly understand the language.
name: Virtuosic Lyrebird

<title level="1" pfs="">[Weremoose](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3323)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A weremoose's tendencies toward stubbornness and a grumpy attitude make it difficult for them to get along with others. Most often, they live as hunters or hermits, taking in the peace of their natural surroundings. If their territory is invaded, they don't hide or retreat but are unlikely to attack. If someone else starts the fight, though, a weremoose retaliates without mercy.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Weremoose.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>

<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=547" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
<trait label="Werecreature" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=733" />
</traits>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 196

**Languages**

**Skills**

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Int** -1

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Deer Empathy** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A weremoose can communicate with deer, including moose.

**Items**
[Greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21), [Hatchet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=27) (2), [Scale Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

[silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) 5

Cold Adaptation The weremoose treats [environmental cold effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) as if they were one step less extreme.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
greataxe +11 ([Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d12+6 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
antler +11,
**Damage** 1d8+6 piercing plus curse of the weremoose

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hatchet +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 slashing

**Damage** 1d6+6 slashing

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Medium human with fist +11 for 1d4+6 bludgeoning, or Large moose with antler and hoof +11 for 1d8+6 bludgeoning.

**Curse of the Weremoose**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Saving Throw DC 17 Fortitude

**Moon Frenzy**  ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Increases antler damage instead of jaws.

**Thundering Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The weremoose Strides twice and then makes an antler Strike. A Medium or smaller creature damaged by this attack must succeed at a DC 17 Fortitude save or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93).

</column>

markdown:
The curse of the wereshark in stills a crude and bloodthirsty attitude, along with an urge to travel the sea. They often become pirates or military sailors who quickly gain a reputation for capricious violence. The shark within makes weresharks born survivors. They will swiftly retreat if they're truly in danger and consider obeying a sense of honor utterly ridiculous. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Wereshark](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3324) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 197 **Perception** +10; blood scent, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 100 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); shark empathy **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +8 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 **Blood Scent** The wereshark can smell blood in the water from up to 1 mile away.
**Shark Empathy** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A wereshark can communicate with sharks. **Items** [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5), [Trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +11 **Will** +8 **HP** 75 **Weaknesses** [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) 5 **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** jaws +14, **Damage** 1d12+7 piercing plus curse of the wereshark **Melee** trident +14, **Damage** 1d8+7 piercing plus fish fork **Ranged** trident +13 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d8+7 piercing **Change Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Medium human with fist +14 for 1d4+7 bludgeoning, or Large shark with jaws +14 for 1d12+7 piercing, no land Speed, and swim Speed 40 feet. The wereshark doesn't have the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=529) trait in human or shark form and has the [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=533) trait in shark form. **Curse of the Wereshark** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) Saving Throw DC 18 Fortitude **Fish Fork** **Requirements** The wereshark critically hit with a trident Strike on their most recent action this turn; **Effect** The wereshark digs their trident deep within their target, skewering it before taking a massive bite. The target of the Strike becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 18) and takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and the wereshark attempts a jaws Strike against it. The wereshark can't use their trident while they have a creature grabbed with it, but they can pull the trident free with a single action that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. **Moon Frenzy** ([Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676))
name: Wereshark

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 54 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 52 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 [Warden of Caverns and Burrows](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3325) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 203 **Perception** +36; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet **Languages** voice of nature **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +42, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +37, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +37, [Subterranean Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +35 **Str** +12 **Dex** +8 **Con** +10 **Int** +6 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +9 **Voice of Nature**
**Warden's Crown** --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +39 **Ref** +36 **Will** +33 +1 to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 500 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=372)), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 20 **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 20 **Magmatic Reflex** **Trigger** The warden is targeted with an [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540); **Effect** The warden issues a spray of magma as they jump away from danger. This spray deals 6d12 fire damage to all adjacent creatures (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). They then [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) or Step away, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to their AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 40 feet, burrow 40 feet **Melee** horned crown +41 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+22 piercing **Melee** mandible +41 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+22 bludgeoning plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Ranged** chirp +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d10+18 sonic **Ranged** magma jet +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+13 fire plus 2d6 persistent fire plus Obsidian Cage **Bury Beneath Stone** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirements** The warden has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77); **Effect** The warden attempts to wedge a creature they have grabbed in their mandibles within a crack in the earth that opens beneath them. The warden attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the grabbed creature's Reflex DC. If they succeed, they bury the creature in the ground. The creature takes 10d10 bludgeoning damage. It is also [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), and has to hold its breath or start [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=2436) until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 42). If the creature is still buried at the end of its turn, it takes 5d10 bludgeoning damage. **Called to Depths** The depths call to the warden, especially as they move through the air. When they [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), they can use any unused movement from the Long Jump to [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310). Additionally, the warden can Burrow through any earthen matter, including rock. When they do so, they move at their full burrow Speed. **Erupting Jump** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The warden draws out a stream of lava, then jumps off the solidifying mass as it cools. The warden [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) up to twice their Speed. When they land, the force of the impact deals 6d12 bludgeoning damage to all creatures within a 15-foot emanation with a DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the creature is also [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet away from the warden. **Obsidian Cage** Rapidly cooling obsidian clings to the target's body and stiffens around their limbs. The target must attempt a DC 42 Reflex save. On a failure, the target is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until they [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 42). On a critical failure, the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for as long as they remain immobilized. **Vibratory Excavation** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The warden screeches with a resonant call that tears apart earth and stone. They create a 10-foot-square, 50-foot-deep pit in earthen material or stone within 60 feet. **Wall Cling** The Warden of Caverns and Burrows clings to surfaces with their segmented feet. They do not need to use a hand to hold on to walls or ceilings.
name: Warden of Caverns and Burrows

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 54 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 52 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 [Warden of Forests and Meadows](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3326) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 205 **Perception** +36; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 1 mile **Languages** voice of nature **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +40, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +44, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +42, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +36, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +39, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +42, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +36, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +40 **Str** +7 **Dex** +10 **Con** +8 **Int** +11 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +12 **Voice of Nature**
**Warden's Crown** --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +42 **Will** +36 +1 to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 435 **Wild Trickery** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** An enemy within 60 feet targets the warden with an [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), spell, or other ability; **Effect** The triggering creature must attempt a DC 45 Will save. If it fails, the warden redirects the ability to target a different creature in range with a burst of mental laughter; this has no effect if there are no other eligible targets. The triggering creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours unless it critically failed. --- **Speed** 60 feet, climb 30 feet; forest passage **Melee** jaws +38 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+15 piercing **Melee** horned crown +40 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+15 piercing plus 2d6 persistent poison **Melee** claw +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+15 slashing **Ranged** fox's laughter +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d10 sonic plus 3d6 mental **Primal Innate Spells** DC 45, attack +41 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **10th** [Manifestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592), [Petal Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=967) - **Constant (7th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728), [Veil of Privacy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The warden transforms his size to Tiny, Small, Medium, or Huge (changing his reach to 0 feet for Tiny, 5 feet for Medium or Large, and 10 feet for Huge). The warden retains his shape but can change his color to any seasonal array. **Forest Passage** The Warden of Forests and Meadows ignores [difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) from non-magical foliage. **Fox's Wager** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The warden grants a boon to a willing creature he can see other than himself, requesting that the creature promise to use the granted power to destroy or undermine the creations of civilization. The target gains a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and a +5 status bonus to damage against [constructs](/Traits.aspx?ID=563), objects, and structures constructed by humanoids. It also gains a +2 status bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks against [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). The warden can have only one wager granted at a time. The wager ends if the target is captured or dies. **Idyllic Panorama** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The warden creates an area of peaceful calm. This is identical to a 5th-rank [_mirage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604) depicting a peaceful meadow or forest, except the area is a 200-foot burst. While within the area of an Idyllic Panorama, the warden has significant control over the senses of creatures within the area and can cast [_mislead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1605) and 2nd-rank [_silence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1674) at will. **Magical Pounce** The warden Strides up to his Speed, [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) up to his Speed, or casts [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724); at the end of that movement, the warden Strikes. If the warden began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), he remains hidden until after this ability's Strike. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** The warden deals an add itional 3d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.
name: Warden of Forests and Meadows

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 54 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 52 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 [Warden of Ocean and Rivers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3327) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 207 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 200 feet **Languages** voice of nature **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +36, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +39, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +42, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +42 **Str** +10 **Dex** +9 **Con** +12 **Int** +6 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +6 **Voice of Nature**
**Warden's Crown** --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +39 **Ref** +36 **Will** +33 +1 to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 540 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision **Resistances** bludgeoning 20, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 20 **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 15 **Bioluminescent Maelstrom** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) 30 feet. The warden's wrath conjures a dazzling storm around them that makes the area [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). Creatures that enter the storm must attempt a DC 45 Will save. A creature that succeeds at this save is temporarily immune to the luring visuals for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round, and for the first action on its next turn, the creature must use a single action to move as close to the warden as possible.
**Critical Failure** The creature is dazzled for as long as it remains in the aura. Whenever the creature begins its turn within the aura, the creature must use the first action of its turn to move as close to the warden as possible. It can attempt a new save at the end of its turn, ending this effect on a success. --- **Speed** swim 100 feet **Melee** horned crown +41 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+15 cold plus 2d8+15 poison **Melee** tentacle +43 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d10+16 bludgeoning plus Grasp of the Deep **All Are One** **Frequency** once per turn; **Effect** The warden attracts organisms that extend their influence. The area of bioluminescent maelstrom and the [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) of their tentacle Strike increase by 10 feet for 1 minute, to a maximum of an additional 30 feet. **Arctic Embrace** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)) All creatures in the warden's bioluminescent maelstrom take 10d6 cold damage (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). On a failure, the creature is also [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 1 round. **Grasp of the Deep** Creatures struck by one of the warden's tentacles gain cold weakness 10 and take a –2 status penalty to Fortitude saves for 1 round. **I Am The Tide** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The warden creates three lines of rushing water, each 10 feet wide and 120 feet long. Creatures in the area take 18d8 bludgeoning damage (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). The warden can either [push or pull](/Rules.aspx?ID=451), and all creatures that fail this save move up to 40 feet in the direction chosen. The warden can't use I Am The Tide again for 1d4 rounds. **What Lurks Beneath** The warden attacks with their tentacles six times.
name: Warden of Ocean and Rivers

**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 54 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 52 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 [Warden of Peaks and Skies](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3328) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 209 **Perception** +40; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 60 feet, stormsight **Languages** voice of nature **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +42, [Bird Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +34, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +38, [Mountain Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +38, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +40, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +38 **Str** +8 **Dex** +12 **Con** +9 **Int** +6 **Wis** +10 **Cha** +8 **Stormsight** Wind, precipitation, and clouds don't impair the warden's vision; she ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from storms, mist, precipitation, and the like.
**Voice of Nature**
**Warden's Crown** --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +40 **Will** +38 +1 to all saves vs [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 445 **Immunities** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15 --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 240 feet; [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** beak +41 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+16 piercing **Melee** horned crown +41 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+16 piercing **Ranged** gale blast +39 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d8+16 bludgeoning plus 2d8 electricity **Primal Innate Spells** DC 45 - **9th** [Soothing Spring](/Spells.aspx?ID=1000) - **10th** [Revival](/Spells.aspx?ID=1654), [Wrathful Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1759) - **Constant (4th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Crystal Exhalation** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The warden breathes, creating a storm of ice crystals. All creatures in a 60-foot cone take 4d8 cold damage and 4d8 slashing damage (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). On a critical failure, a creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) by ice. The warden then can't use Crystal Exhalation for 1d4 rounds. **Orogenesis Dive** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The warden flies 240 feet upward in a straight line. As she does so, the ground in a 30-foot burst from her starting point rises into a 120-foot-high rocky pillar topped with a mountainous peak that becomes [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453). All other creatures in that burst must succeed at a DC 44 Fortitude save or be [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) by the sudden altitude shift. The warden then flies straight downward to the center of the pillar's peak, driving her horns into the stone. As she lands, the newly formed mountains tremble. All other creatures in the area must succeed at a DC 42 Reflex save or plummet off the peaks, [falling](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) 120 feet and landing in the nearest square outside the area. The pillar is permanent. **Song of the Skies** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The warden trills a cheerful birdsong, granting a boon to all allies within 60 feet and evoking the freedom of flight. For the next minute, affected allies gain [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732), a [fly speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 60 feet, and treat creatures on the ground as [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) when they are airborne. **Tempest Downdrafts** ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Trigger** The warden [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312); **Effects** The warden's flapping wings generate powerful gusts that blow smaller creatures off course. A 60-foot emanation, centered on the warden, fills with lightning-pierced winds until the beginning of the warden's next turn. Creatures must succeed at a DC 42 Reflex save to take any [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action within the winds; flying creatures use the outcome one degree of success worse than what they roll. Creatures who critically fail are [blown](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 30 feet away from the warden, knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), and take 4d6 electricity damage.
Warden's Blessing Legend says that the Warden of Peaks and Sky invites those who particularly earn their favor into their home, a nest within a hollow peak deep in the Kodar Mountains. There, the guest is treated to a grand feast, after which the warden offers to turn them into a [thunderbird](/Monsters.aspx?ID=825) so they might enjoy the sky for the rest of their days, though they are not offended if anyone demurs.
name: Warden of Peaks and Skies

jaws +12 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 piercing plus ghoul fever and paralysis

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 slashing plus paralysis

**Divine Spells Prepared** DC 21, attack +13
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)

**Consume Flesh** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The ghoul is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour. **Effect** The ghoul devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 3d6 Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once.

**Ghoul Fever**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 15; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 2d6 negative damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 2d6 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a ghoul the next midnight.

**Paralysis**  ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living, non-elf creature hit by a ghoul's attack must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85). It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save.

**Swift Leap** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The ghoul jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

</column>
name: Glorkus

**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Divine Warden Of Haagenti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3332) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 20 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12 **Str** +3 **Dex** -2 **Con** +2 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +7 **Will** +9 **HP** 45 **Divine Destruction** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the divine warden is reduced to 0 HP, it erupts with divine energy in a 30-foot emanation, dealing 4d6 positive damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 17 Will save with the following outcomes.
**Faith Bound** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The divine warden can't attack a creature that openly wears or displays Brigh's religious symbol unless that creature uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the divine warden first.
 --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** battle axe +12 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 2d8+5 slashing **Melee** horns +12 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+5 piercing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84) **Divine Domain Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 17 - **2nd** [Athletic Rush](/Spells.aspx?ID=402), [Splash of Art](/Spells.aspx?ID=453) **Faithful Weapon** [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16), [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) rune **Instrument of Faith** The divine warden is a beacon for its deity's faith. A [cleric](/Traits.aspx?ID=554) of the divine warden's patron deity can channel a [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) spell through a divine warden they can see within 60 feet. The cleric determines any targets or area for the spell as if they were standing in the divine warden's space.
name: Divine Warden Of Haagenti

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Wolgur Wrabs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3333) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 32 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +9, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Channel Protection Amulet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=421) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +11 **Will** +10 **HP** 75 (negative healing) **Immunities** death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, unconscious **Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664)) 10 feet. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 19 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (plus [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). While within the aura, the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against disease and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
 --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 5 feet **Melee** jaws +14 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing plus ghast fever and paralysis **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+6 slashing plus paralysis **Consume Flesh** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The ghast is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour. **Effect** The ghast devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 4d6 Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once. **Ghast Fever** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 22; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 3d8 negative damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 3d8 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a ghast the next midnight **Paralysis** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living creature (including elves) hit by a ghast's attack must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85). It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save. **Swift Leap** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The ghast jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.
name: Wolgur Wrabs

**Distracting Whistle** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Tunch whistles in a way that causes the sound to manifest elsewhere, potentially distracting a foe. One target of Tunch's choice that he can see within 30 feet must attempt a DC 22 Will save or become [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of Tunch's next turn by the distracting whistling sound that seems to come from behind them. The target is then temporarily immune to Distracting Whistle for 1 hour.

**Quick Bomber** <actions string="Single Action" />  Tunch Interacts to draw a bomb, then Strikes with it.
