**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Gurlunk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3335) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 56 **Perception** +11; darkvision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +9 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Items** [dogslicer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1675), _+1 [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75)_ (20 arrows), [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=5) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +12 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 **Goblin Scuttle** **Trigger** A goblin ally ends a move action adjacent to the warrior** **Effect** Gurlunk Steps. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dogslicer +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=81)), **Damage** 1d6+2 slashing **Ranged** shortbow + 13 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **Disorienting Shot** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Gurlunk makes two Strikes against his hunted prey (see below) with his shortbow. If both hit the same creature, the creature succeed at a DC 21 Will save or become [flat- footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) until the start of their next turn (or the start of Gurlunk's next turn on a critical failure). **Hunt Prey** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) Gurlunk designates a single creature he can see or hear, and gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) and [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) that prey. He can only have one creature so designated at a time, and if he selects a new hunted prey, the previous designation ends. **Hunter's Edge** Gurlunk deals an additional 1d8 precision damage the first time he hits his hunted prey in a round. **Whistling Threat** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Gurlunk whistles sharply at a Medium or larger animal or beast he can see within 30 feet, using his voice to dull the creature's wits and cause it to lower its defenses. The target must attempt a DC 21 Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The target is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The target is frightened 3 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round as it shakes in fear.
name: Gurlunk

[darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +12, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +11 **Str** +0 **Dex** +2 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Items** [+1 sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=9), [_clockwork songbird_ key](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3442) --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +10 **Will** +13 **HP** 92 **Goblin Scuttle** **Trigger** A goblin ally ends a move action adjacent to the warrior** **Effect** Minargul Steps. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** sickle +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d4+2 slashing **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 24, attack +14 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=15), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) **Hexes** 2 Focus Points, DC 24 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Stoke the Heart](/Spells.aspx?ID=807) - **3rd** [Needle of Vengeance](/Spells.aspx?ID=801) **Patron** [fervor](/Patrons.aspx?ID=4); **Familiar** Whistlewing (bat) **Whistle Up Cursed Flames** (curse, divine, evocation, fire) Minargul whistles a high-pitched shrill note, breathing out a blast of fire and foul-smelling smoke as she does so. All creatures in a 15-foot-cone must attempt a DC 24 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 3d6 fire damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 6d6 fire damage and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d6 fire damage and is cursed. The burns caused by the fire linger, and the charred flesh remains blistered. The cursed creature takes a –1 status penalty to all checks from the constant pain. Healing doesn't alter the appearance of the burns or lessen the penalty, but removing the curse does.
name: Minargul

**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Ripnugget](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3337) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 63 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [dogslicer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=405)_ --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +12 **Will** +11 **HP** 110 (negative healing, rejuvenation) **Immunities** death effects, disease, fire, paralyzed, poison, unconscious **Sacrilegious Aura** 30 feet ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) 30 feet. When a creature in the aura uses a vitality spell or ability, the graveknight automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) it, with the listed counteract modifier, with a +13 counteract modifier.
[**Attack of Opportunity**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)
--- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dogslicer +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172), [Goblin](/Traits.aspx?ID=81)), **Damage** 2d6+4 slashing plus 1d6 fire and 1d6 bleed **Devastating Blast** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The graveknight unleashes a 30-foot cone of energy. Creatures in the area take 4d12 fire damage (DC 24 basic Reflex save). The graveknight can use this ability once every 1d4 rounds. **Dogslicer Cruelty** On a critical hit with a dogslicer, Ripnugget yanks bits of flesh from his victim as he rips the blade free. The target becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 from the pain, and is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) as long as they remain sickened. **Graveknight's Curse** This curse affects anyone who wears a graveknight's armor for at least 1 hour. **Saving Throw** DC 24 Will save; **Onset** 1 hour; **Stage 1** [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 2** doomed 2, hampered 10, and cannot remove the armor (1 day); **Stage 3** dies and transforms into the armor's graveknight. **Phantom Mount** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33)) he graveknight summons a supernatural mount as per [_phantom steed_](/Spells.aspx?ID=221), heightened to a level equal to half the graveknight's level. Unlike phantom steed, the steed's AC and saving throw bonuses are all 4 lower than the graveknight's, and the steed has one-third the graveknight's Hit Points (rounded down). If the steed is destroyed, the graveknight must wait 1 hour before using this ability again. Ripnugget's mount is a [giant gecko](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3087) (HP 37; AC 19, Fort +11, Ref +8, Will +7) named Stickfoot. **Weapon Master** The graveknight has access to the critical specialization effects of any weapons it wields.
name: Ripnugget

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Devil's Disciple](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3338) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 69 **Perception** +5 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +6, [Sandpoint Devil Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +4, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +5, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +6 **Str** +3 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 **Items** [daggers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (4), [Machete](/Weapons.aspx?ID=95), [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +6 **Will** +7 **HP** 30 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** machete +10 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 1d6+4 slashing **Ranged** dagger +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Devil's Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The Devil's Disciple cuts their tongue with a slashing weapon held in a hand (taking 1 slashing damage), then exhales a 15-foot cone of burning smoke. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 1d8 fire damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 2d8 fire damage and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 4d8 fire damage, is sickened 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns, and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 by the smoke for 1 minute.
name: Devil's Disciple

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Gorlak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3339) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 71 **Perception** +14; [_darkvision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Goblin](/Languages.aspx?ID=6), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13 **Str** +7 **Dex** +0 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Items** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), _+1 [ogre hook](/Weapons.aspx?ID=86)_ --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +11 **Will** +14 **HP** 130 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** ogre hook +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing **Embed Hook** **Trigger** Gorlak's previous action was a successful Strike on a creature with his ogre hook; **Effect** Gorlak twists his hook to embed it in the creature by attempting an Athletics check against the target's Fortitude DC. On a success, that creature takes 1d10+9 piercing damage (DC 25 basic Fortitude save) and becomes [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) until Gorlak attempts another Strike with his Ogre Hook or attempts to Fling Foe the creature. **Fling Foe** **Requirements** Gorlak's last action was to Embed Hook in a Medium or smaller creature; **Effect** Gorlak attempts to fling the creature embedded on his hook through the air by attempting an Athletics check against the embedded creature's Fortitude DC. **Critical Success** The creature takes 2d10+9 piercing damage (DC 25 basic Fortitude save) as the hook rips free and is hurled 15 feet away, becoming [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) in a space of Gorlak's choosing.
**Success** The creature takes 1d10+9 piercing damage (DC 25 basic Fortitude save) as the hook rips free and is hurled 5 feet away, becoming prone.
**Failure** The creature takes 1d10 piercing damage (DC 25 basic Fortitude save) as the hook rips free and is left standing in a space adjacent to Gorlak.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes no damage as the hook rips free. It is left standing in a space adjacent to Gorlak, and Gorlak becomes so frustrated by his failure that he must use his first action on his next turn to curse and scream and shake his fists in the air.
name: Gorlak

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Asmerelli](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3340) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 73 **Perception** +13 **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15, [Sandpoint Devil Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +13 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Machete](/Weapons.aspx?ID=95), [religious symbol](/Armor.aspx?ID=22), _+1 [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=437)_ (20 arrows) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +15 **Will** +15 **HP** 92 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** machete +10 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 1d6+2 slashing **Ranged** _flaming shortbow_ +16 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing plus 1d6 fire **Divine Spontaneous Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Flaming Sphere](/Spells.aspx?ID=121), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104), [Searing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=269), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) (4 slots) **Sorcerer Bloodline Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 24 - **3rd** [Diabolic Edict](/Spells.aspx?ID=498), [Embrace the Pit](/Spells.aspx?ID=506) **Bloodline** [diabolic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=4) **Devil's Cursed Breath** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Asmerelli bites down on her tongue (taking 1 slashing damage), then exhales a 15-foot cone of burning smoke. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 24 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 3d8 fire damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 6d8 fire damage and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d8 fire damage, is sickened 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns, and becomes cursed. The victim's burns never fully vanish, and the cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to all attacks from the constant pain. Healing doesn't alter the appearance of the burns or lessen the penalty, but removing the curse does. **Reach Spell** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) If the next action Asmerelli takes is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell's range by 30 feet.
name: Asmerelli

**Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language)1

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +6

**Con** +4

**Cha** +0

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d6+11 slashing

**Chupar** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The chupacabra has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77); **Effect** The chupacabra sucks blood from the grabbed creature. The chupacabra gains the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition for 1 minute and can use the extra action only for Strike and Stride actions. A chupacabra can't use Chupar again while it is quickened in this way. A creature that has its blood drained by a chupacabra is [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 until it receives healing (of any kind or amount).

**Pounce** <actions string="Single Action" />  The chupacabra Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the chupacabra began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), it remains hidden until after this ability's Strike.

**Wing Flurry** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The false devil's spiny wings don't afford it a typical melee option, but it can use them to scrape and slash at foes it flies by. The winged chupacabra attempts to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) an enemy's space. As it does so, it flaps its wings furiously, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to its Acrobatics check. If it succeeds, it inflicts 4d6 slashing damage to the creature it Tumbled Through (or 8d6 slashing damage on a critical success).

</column>
name: False Devil

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Iazmilor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3342) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 89 **Perception** +16; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), echolocation (precise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Grioth](/Languages.aspx?ID=65); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Echolocation** A grioth can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) voidglass [warhammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=403)_, [_wand of blindness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1453) --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +16 **Will** +18 **HP** 145 **Immunities** cold **Weaknesses** fire 10 **Light Blindness** No Breath A grioth doesn't breathe except to speak and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)).
 --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** _warhammer_ +17 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 2d8+6 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing plus grioth venom **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=18), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **2nd** [Create Food](/Spells.aspx?ID=52), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=287), [Spiritual Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 23, attack +15 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **4th** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) **Grim Curse of Azathoth** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental **Frequency** once per day](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Effect** Ziradini invokes Azathoth's name in Aklo as he stares at a creature he can see within 30 feet. His eyes glow orange, and the target endures a brief vision of the seething chaos that churns at the center of the Material Plane and, for that instant, beholds their own insignificance in the cosmos. The target must attempt a DC 26 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 hour.
**Failure** The creature becomes stupefied 1, and the first time each night they gaze upon the night sky, they must make a DC 26 Will save or they increase the value of their stupefied condition by 1 (to a maximum of stupefied 4, or they become stupefied 1 if they do not have this condition). Even if the stupefied condition is removed, the target must continue to save upon gazing upon the night sky once per night until the curse is removed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target feels the weight of the cosmos when the sun sets on their homeland, wherever they may be in the Material Plane or the Great Beyond—they need not gaze upon the night sky. **Grioth Venom** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 17; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** frightened 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** frightened 3 (1 round) **Shock Mind** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The grioth scout makes a Strike with a voidglass weapon. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional 4d6 mental damage, and the target must succeed at a DC 26 Will save (this has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait) or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
name: Iazmilor

**Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will) - **3rd** [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) - **4th** [Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=18), [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [Sanctuary](/Spells.aspx?ID=266) - **2nd** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×2), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=287), [Spiritual Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) - **3rd** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36), [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Vampiric Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=354) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×5), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) **Cleric Domain Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 26 - **4th** [Cry of Destruction](/Spells.aspx?ID=410) **Grim Curse of Azathoth** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Ziradini invokes Azathoth's name in Aklo as he stares at a creature he can see within 30 feet. His eyes glow orange, and the target endures a brief vision of the seething chaos that churns at the center of the Material Plane and, for that instant, beholds their own insignificance in the cosmos. The target must attempt a DC 26 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 hour.
**Failure** The creature becomes stupefied 1, and the first time each night they gaze upon the night sky, they must make a DC 26 Will save or they increase the value of their stupefied condition by 1 (to a maximum of stupefied 4, or they become stupefied 1 if they do not have this condition). Even if the stupefied condition is removed, the target must continue to save upon gazing upon the night sky once per night until the curse is removed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target feels the weight of the cosmos when the sun sets on their homeland, wherever they may be in the Material Plane or the Great Beyond—they need not gaze upon the night sky.
name: Ziradini

Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 22 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d8 fire damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 4d8 fire damage and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 6d8 fire damage, is sickened 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns, and is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 by the smoke for 1 minute. **Sneak Attack** The ascended disciple deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
name: Ascended Disciple

**4th** [Inspire Heroics](/Spells.aspx?ID=388), [Lingering Composition](/Spells.aspx?ID=389) **Devil's Cursed Breath** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Jordus bites down on his tongue (taking 1 slashing damage), then exhales a 15-foot cone of burning smoke. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 26 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 3d8 fire damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 7d8 fire damage and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns.
**Critical Failure** he creature takes 10d8 fire damage, is sickened 1 by the agonizing pain of the burns, and becomes cursed. The victim's burns never fully vanish, and the cursed creature takes a –2 status penalty to all attacks from the constant pain. Healing doesn't alter the appearance of the burns or lessen the penalty, but removing the curse does.
name: Jordus Munt

Mnoghoth can suppress or activate this aura as a single action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.

**[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Zalavexus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3349) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 113 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22), [Thassilonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=52), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +21, [Genie Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +19, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +21 **Str** +3 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 30 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +17 **Ref** +21 **Will** +19 **HP** 148 (fast healing 10) **Immunities** disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, swarm mind, unconscious **Resistances** physical 10 **Weaknesses** area damage 10, splash damage 10 **Discorporate** When the worm that walks is reduced to 0 HP, it discorporates and the component worms that make up its body disperse in every direction. If even a single worm escapes, the worm that walks will eventually re-form using a process that typically takes 1d10 days. While the exact circumstances and surroundings determine how long the worm that walks's foes have to dispatch the fleeing worms before they escape, usually its foes have only a single round. Typically, this requires the application of an area effect or splash weapon within 1 round to the space where the worm that walks collapsed. After any amount of area or splash damage is dealt to that space, the character dealing the damage must attempt a DC 15 flat check. Each subsequent area or splash damage effect performed on the area reduces the DC of this flat check by 2, to a minimum of DC 5. If any of these flat checks succeed, none of the worms escape, and the worm that walks is destroyed permanently. At the GM's discretion, clever means of trapping or otherwise detaining the vermin may extend the time allowed to finish off the worm that walks.
 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** tendril +21 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+6 persistent piercing damage **Primal Spells Known** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330) - **1st** [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140), [Longstrider](/Spells.aspx?ID=175) - **2nd** [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Spider Climb](/Spells.aspx?ID=299), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94), [Meld into Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=188), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=366) - **4th** [Acid Arrow](/Spells.aspx?ID=2), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276) - **5th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=365) **Sorcerer Bloodline Spells** 1 Focus Point, DC 29, attack +21 - **5th** [Elemental Toss](/Spells.aspx?ID=505) **Quickened Casting** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** If Zalavexus's next action is to cast a 3rd- or lower-level spell or cantrip, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action). **Squirming Embrace** The worm that walks Strides, ending its movement sharing a space with a creature, and deals 4d8 piercing damage to the creature. The creature can attempt a DC 27 basic Reflex save. **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt Zalavexus's spellcasting action, he attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted. **Swarm Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The worm that walks collapses into a shapeless swarm of worms. It drops all held, worn, and carried items. While discorporated, the worm that walks can't use attack actions and can't cast spells, but it can move through areas small enough for its individual worms to fit without having to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372). It can use the same action to coalesce back into its normal form.
name: Zalavexus

**Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning

**Brutal Brawler**   Chertus can choose to inflict lethal damage with his fists, and increases the size of his damage die with fist attacks to 1d6. If he does so, on a critical hit, the target struck must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 by the staggering blow.

**Exacting Strike** <actions string="Single Action" />  (press) Chertus makes a Strike. If he rolls a failure on this Strike, the attack doesn't count toward his multiple attack penalty.

**Lunge** <actions string="Single Action" />  Chertus makes a Strike with his fist and increases his reach by 5 feet for this Strike.

**Pummeling Flurry** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Chertus swings his fist in a pummeling arc, then makes a fist Strike and compares the attack roll result to the AC of up to two adjacent foes. He then rolls damage and applies it to each creature he hits. This counts as two attacks for his multiple attack penalty.

**Snatch Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" />  Chertus attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) a creature. If he knocks a weapon out of the opponent's grasp, Chertus wields the weapon and attempts a Strike against the opponent with the weapon. This counts as two attacks for his multiple attack penalty.

</column>
name: Chertus Jheed

**Damage** 2d6+5 slashing

**Damage** 1d6+5 piercing

**Change Shape** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) Human with fist +18 for 1d4+5 bludgeoning, or Small rat with Speed 30 feet, climb Speed 10 feet

**Curse of the Wererat**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) This curse affects only humanoids. Saving Throw Fortitude DC 26. On each full moon, the cursed creature must succeed at another Fortitude save or turn into the same kind of werecreature until dawn. The creature is under the GM's control and goes on a rampage for half the night before falling unconscious until dawn.

**Moon Frenzy**   (polymorph, primal, transmutation); When a full moon appears in the night sky, the werecreature must enter hybrid form, can't Change Shape thereafter, becomes one size larger, increases its reach by 5 feet, and increases the damage of its jaws Strike (or a similar Strike) by 2. When the moon sets or the sun rises, the werecreature returns to humanoid form and is fatigued for 2d4 hours.

**Sneak Attack**   Adimar's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to flat-footed creatures.

**Unexpected Transformation** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirement** Adimar is in human or rat form; **Effect** Adimar uses Change Shape to transform into his hybrid form and attempts to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) one target within 30 feet, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to his Intimidation check to do so. All creatures who witness Adimar use Unexpected Transformation can't be Demoralized by future Unexpected Transformations for 24 hours.

</column>

**[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 38 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Jervis Stoot](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3354) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 131 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +21, [Sandpoint Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +22 **Str** -5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Trinket Bound** Jervis Stoot can normally stray only a short distance from the underground chambers atop Chopper's Isle just north of Sandpoint, but when the head of the _[clockwork songbird](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3442)_ is brought out of that area, Stoot can follow. As long as a creature he possesses carries the head, he can travel as he wishes, but no further than 5 miles from the town of Sandpoint.
 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +22 **Will** +21 **HP** 150 (negative healing, rejuvenation) **Immunities** blindness, death effects, disease, paralyzed, poison, precision, unconscious, visual effects **Resistances** all damage 10 (except force, ghost touch, positive, or Strikes from birds or avian creatures [including creatures who fly with wings, but not creatures who fly through magic alone]; double resistance vs. non-magical) **Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If Jervis is destroyed, he rejuvenates back in the underground chambers below Chopper's Isle and seeks out the carrier of the clockwork songbird's head as long as they remain within 5 miles of Sandpoint. If the architect of his whole doom, the Red Bishop, is slain, Stoot can move on to the afterlife.
 --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Melee** ghostly hatchet +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+11 negative plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)persistent bleed **Ghostly Murder** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Jervis Stoot raises a hand high, and a murder of ghostly crows manifests in a vortex above his hand. He then lowers his hand to point in a direction, and the murder of ghostly crows swarms out in a 20-foot cone. All creatures caught in the area must attempt a DC 30 Reflex save. Jervis can't use Ghostly Murder again for 1d4 rounds. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 6d6 slashing damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 10d6 slashing damage and 2d6 [persistent bleed damage.](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29)
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 15d6 slashing damage, 5d6 persistent bleed damage, and is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59). The blindness persists until it's healed via an effect like [_restore senses_](/Spells.aspx?ID=259). **Malevolent Possession** he ghost attempts to possess an adjacent corporeal creature, but only against a creature that carries the head of the clockwork songbird somewhere on their person. This has the same effect as the [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1638) spell, except since the ghost doesn't have a physical body, it is unaffected by that restriction of the spell. **Tears of Blood** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Jervis Stoot makes a plucking motion with his fingers. One creature he can sense within 30 feet experiences the agony of their eyes being plucked out and takes 12d6 mental damage from the pain (DC 30 basic Fortitude save, plus [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute on a critical failure).

 --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 5 feet **Melee** _flail_ +18 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=175), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning **Melee** jaws +17 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d8+10 piercing plus ghast fever and paralysis **Melee** claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d6+10 slashing plus paralysis **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 25, attack +17 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) - **2nd** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=287), [Spiritual Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=306) - **3rd** [Blindness](/Spells.aspx?ID=26), [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) - **4th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=86), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) **Consume Flesh** **Requirements** The ghast is adjacent to the corpse of a creature that died within the last hour. **Effect** The ghast devours a chunk of the corpse and regains 5d6 Hit Points. It can regain Hit Points from any given corpse only once. A ghast cultist can also scoop up a handful of maggots to feed upon if they're adjacent to the large bowl in the middle of area **J1**. **Ghast Fever** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 25; **Stage 1** carrier with no ill effect (1 day); **Stage 2** 3d8 negative damage and regains half as many Hit Points from all healing (1 day); **Stage 3** as stage 2 (1 day); **Stage 4** 3d8 negative damage and gains no benefit from healing (1 day); **Stage 5** as stage 4 (1 day); **Stage 6** dead, and rises as a ghast the next midnight **Paralysis** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living creature (including elves) hit by a ghast's attack must succeed at a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85). It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each such save. **Swift Leap** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) The ghast jumps up to half its Speed. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.
name: Ghast Cultists

[darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 30 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +20, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +20 **Str** +3 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +7 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +24 **Will** +23 **HP** 221 --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** talon +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 3d10+6 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **2nd** [Gentle Repose](/Spells.aspx?ID=131), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only), [Paranoia](/Spells.aspx?ID=214) (at will) - **3rd** [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=291) (×3) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Globe of Invulnerability](/Spells.aspx?ID=137) **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Unseen Servant](/Spells.aspx?ID=352) - **2nd** [Hideous Laughter](/Spells.aspx?ID=150), [Humanoid Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=153), [Misdirection](/Spells.aspx?ID=198) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **5th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=365) - **6th** [Baleful Polymorph](/Spells.aspx?ID=17), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Flesh to Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=123) **Body Thief** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129)) The intellect devourer reduces in size and burrows into the brain of a creature dead for no longer than 1 day. At the start of the intellect devourer's next turn, the body revives at its maximum Hit Points, controlled by the devourer. The intellect devourer is conscious and can sense everything the possessed body could. Any effect that ends the possession kills the host body with the same effects as Exit Body. The intellect devourer can't use any of the host creature's spells with Body Thief but can use its own spells. The host body slowly decays while inhabited by the intellect devourer, becoming uninhabitable after 7 days unless preserved with [_gentle repose_](/Spells.aspx?ID=131) or a similar effect. **Exit Body** ([Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114)) **Requirements** The intellect devourer is controlling a body with Body Thief; **Effect** The intellect devourer leaves its host body, which dies instantly and is no longer a suitable host for any Body Thief ability. The intellect devourer appears at full size in an adjacent space. **Nightmare Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Tiluatchek reaches out with his mind to infect the dreams of a single creature within 60 feet that he can see. The target suddenly experiences a brief but overwhelming memory of a harrowing nightmare it had at some point in the past and must attempt a DC 32 Will save. The effects of this curse are permanent until the target is cured (although any frightened condition values imparted by the curse pass and end normally). **Critical Success** The target is unaffected, and the nightmare vision passes as swiftly as it appears.
**Success** The vision causes the target to become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. The target also becomes cursed. Every time it attempts to sleep, it's plagued by nightmares of being stalked through an endless maze of caverns by something that wants to steal its body and eat its brain. The cursed creature now requires 10 hours of rest (rather than 8) in order to be able to make its daily preparations and can't take the Long-Term Rest activity at all.
**Failure** As success, but frightened 2, and each time the target sleeps, it suffers the effects of a [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) spell (DC 32 Will save).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but frightened 3 and fleeing for as long as it remains frightened. If the cursed target ever rolls a critical failure against a _nightmare_ spell while it remains cursed (including those caused by the curse itself), it dies in its sleep. If at the time of this death Tiluatchek is also dead (but for no more than 1 year), the intellect devourer is restored to life inside of the target creature's body as if he'd used Body Thief to take the body over. **Ravage** The intellect devourer makes two talon Strikes against a [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90), or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) creature, using the same attack modifier as its highest attack modifier. These Strikes gain the death trait. If Ravage kills the target, the intellect devourer may use Body Thief against it as a free action. **Stolen Identity** While an intellect devourer uses Body Thief, it gains the ability to understand and speak all languages known by the host, as well as knowledge of the host body's abilities, identity, role in society, and personality. However, it does not gain the specific memories or knowledge of the host body.
name: Tiluatchek

**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Scarhorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3359) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 152 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Thassilonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=52), [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=44) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +24, [Thassilon Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +22 **Will** +24 **HP** 275 **Immunities** acid, paralyzed, sleep [Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) (aura, emotion, fear, mental) 90 feet, DC 31
**Tail Lash** **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's tail takes an action to Strike or attempt a skill check** **Efiect** The dragon Strikes with its tail at the triggering creature at a –2 penalty. If it hits, the creature takes a –2 circumstance penalty to the triggering roll. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 120 feet, swim 50 feet **Melee** jaws +29 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+13 piercing plus 2d6 acid **Melee** claw +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+13 slashing **Melee** tail +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning **Melee** horns +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+11 piercing **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 34, attack +26 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=7), [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [True Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=345) - **2nd** [Comprehend Language](/Spells.aspx?ID=46), [Humanoid Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=153), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) - **3rd** [Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=22), [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173), [Shrink Item](/Spells.aspx?ID=285) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=51), [Mariner's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=184), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) - **6th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=268) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 34 - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will) **Breath Weapon** ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) Scarhorn breathes a spray of acid that deals 15d6 acid damage in an 80-foot line (DC 34 basic Reflex save). She can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Corrupt Water** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon permanently befouls 10 cubic feet of liquid within 90 feet. The liquid becomes undrinkable and unable to support water-breathing life. This destroys liquid magic or alchemical items if they're of a lower level than the dragon (a creature can attempt a DC 31 Will save to protect liquids in its possession). This doesn't affect liquids in a creature's body.
name: Scarhorn

fire 5

**Final Blasphemy** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) When the herexen is destroyed, it explodes in a wave of void energy with the effects of a 3-action 4th level [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) spell (DC 26). The herexen is destroyed, so it doesn't gain any Hit Points from this use of harm, and it doesn't need to have any harm spells remaining to use this ability.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
staff +16 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning

**Divine Spontaneous Spells** DC 26, attack +18
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280)
- **1st**
[Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=45), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244) (3 slots)
- **2nd**
[Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=256), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=287) (3 slots)
- **3rd**
[Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36), [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=55), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) (3 slots)
- **4th**
[Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) (3 slots)

**Revelation Spells** DC 26, 2 Focus Points
- **4th**
[Tempest Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=763), [Thunderburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=764)

**Swarming Curse**   Hundreds of biting flies crawl over Narlo's bones, filling out his armor almost like his flesh once did. When these flies burn, they harm Narlo as well, imparting weakness to fire 5. Whenever Narlo casts a spell, these flies burst from his body to fill a 10- foot emanation around him in a buzzing, biting cloud until the start of his next turn. During this time, he loses his weakness to fire, and any creature that ends their turn within the emanation takes 4d6 piercing damage (DC 23 basic Reflex save) from the biting flies.

</column>

Clockwork serpents are often found serving as guardians in serpentfolk laboratories or temples. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Clockwork Serpent](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3362) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 188 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** -5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -5 **Wind-Up** 24 hours, DC 26, standby --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +16 **HP** 110 **Immunities** death effects, disease, doomed, drained, fatigued, healing, mental, necromancy, nonlethal attacks, paralyzed, poisoned, sickened, unconscious **Resistances** physical 10 (except adamantine or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** electricity 10, orichalcum 10 **Critical Deactivation** If a clockwork serpent takes any precision damage or takes a critical hit and it has a serpent mode active (see below), it must attempt a DC 16 flat check. On a failure, it takes the damage from the critical hit as normal, is stunned 1, and its serpent mode deactivates.
Activate Serpent Mode** **Trigger** The clockwork serpent begins its turn** **Effect** The clockwork serpent transforms into a different mode. Roll 1d6 and consult the options below. If it rolls a mode it can't currently activate, the clockwork serpent becomes clumsy 1 until the start of its next turn. At the end of its turn, it automatically deactivates its serpent mode.
**1–2 Cobra Mode** The clockwork serpent can use Intimidating Hood.
**3–4 Constrictor Mode** The clockwork serpent can use its coils Strike and can Constrict.
**5–6 Rattlesnake Mode** The clockwork serpent can use Shattering Rattle. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing plus clockwork serpent venom **Melee** coils +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d12+9 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45); **Requirements** The clockwork serpent is in constrictor mode **Clockwork Serpent Venom** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; Maximum Duration 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage, [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1, and flat-footed (1 round) **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d10+6 bludgeoning; DC 26; **Requirements** The clockwork serpent is in constrictor mode **Intimidating Hood** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Requirements** The clockwork serpent is in cobra mode; **Effect** The clockwork serpent flares out a cobra hood. All creatures within a 15-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure). **Shattering Rattle** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) **Requirements** The clockwork serpent is in rattlesnake mode; **Effect** The clockwork serpent rattles its tail, then snaps it like a whip to create a devastating blast of sonic energy in a 30-foot cone. Creatures in the area take 9d6 sonic damage (DC 26 basic Fort save).
Experimental Serpents Even stranger clockwork serpents are rumored to exist in the heart of serpentfolk enclaves. These constructs supposedly can repair damage to themselves when they change serpent modes, activate additional forms that allow swimming or flight, or even break apart into a seething swarm of smaller clockwork serpents.
name: Clockwork Serpent

Although the coloxus has the hideous head of a monstrous fly, these demons are fastidious and clean, always dressing in stylish clothing without a blemish on them. Coloxuses are manipulators and schemers born from destructively vain souls. Evil mortals are fond of conjuring them to serve as emissaries or spies, but they also make excellent assassins. Even in the role of murderer, the demon remains cloyingly polite and formal, reveling in the irony of perpetuating such a horrible crime under the guise of good etiquette, as if it were a delicate bit of diplomacy. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Coloxus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3363) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 189 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +23 **Str** +5 **Dex** +7 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +25 **Will** +18 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 270 **Immunities** disease **Resistances** poison 10 **Weaknesses** cold iron 10, good 10 **Filth Vulnerability** A coloxus despises contact with filth and dirt. A small amount of filth held in the hand can be used against a coloxus as a Strike that has the thrown 5 feet trait, and that inflicts 2d6 mental damage on a hit. Forcing a coloxus to fall into or become immersed in filth deals 6d6 mental damage per round.
**Vanishing Words** **Trigger** The coloxus rolls initiative with Deception or Diplomacy and can cast invisibility **Effect** The coloxus casts 4th-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) on itself. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** jaws +25 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+8 piercing plus 1d6 evil and siphon **Divine Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **5th** [Abyssal Plague](/Spells.aspx?ID=1), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) - **6th** [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=273), [Summon Animal](/Spells.aspx?ID=316) (giant arthropods only) - **Constant (6th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Droning Wings** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The coloxus flutters its shimmering, gauzy wings and creates a mesmerizing display of color along with a sound-dampening buzzing drone. If the coloxus is invisible, that effect ends and it becomes visible. All creatures within a 30-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (slowed 2 on a critical failure) until the start of the coloxus's next turn. As long as Droning Wings continues, the coloxus gains immunity to sonic damage. The coloxus can Sustain Droning Wings for up to 1 minute. **Siphon** When a coloxus damages a creature with its jaws Strike, it siphons away the target's physical vitality and drinks from their sense of self-worth and confidence. The coloxus gains 10 temporary Hit Points, and the creature must succeed at a DC 32 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1. Further damage dealt by the coloxus increases the stupefied condition by 1 on a failed save to a maximum of stupefied 4. **[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** A coloxus deals 2d6 extra precision damage to flat-footed creatures.
Enemies Of Filth Filth is often inescapable in the Abyss, and as a result, coloxus demons are particularly eager to be conjured away from that plane to work their evils in cleaner realities. When one uses [_planar ally_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=16) or [_planar binding_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=17) to conjure a coloxus, summoning it into a clean environment and promising you won't compel it to serve under filthy conditions may make the demon easier to bargain with. At the GM's discretion, the demon may agree to serve you for longer or at a lower cost than normal.
name: Coloxus

markdown:
Hundreds of years ago, a cadre of powerful succubus assassins invaded the Abyssal realm of the demon lord of disease and fungus, Cyth-V'sug. These succubi sought to slay a powerful witch who had befouled a profane temple the assassins served. The succubi failed at their mission, but from their corpses grew the first fungus tyrants. Pleased with this development, the witch then seeded numerous worlds on the Material Plane with fungus tyrant spores, who have since grown and thrived in their new environments as rot thrives in a corpse. Today, hundreds of fungus tyrants inhabit the fouler locales of the Material Plane. They're particularly fond of large cave systems and ruined cities, but also can be found in corrupted woodlands where blighted fey rule or in the sewers of active cities that spew vast amounts of magical waste.
An encounter with a normally unintelligent plant monster acting with methodical intent can be evidence of a fungus tyrant's manipulations, but fungus tyrants are equally fond of keeping other types of creatures as pets and thralls—particularly attractive humanoids. Especially favored minions are given the gift of the fungus tyrant's embrace and are transformed into fungoid minions forever loyal to their pallid ruler. Yet most fungus tyrants realize that a diverse array of guardians is best, and they often choose to keep some non-fungus minions on hand to protect their lair.
Fungus tyrants are violently jealous. Any who attempt to lure away their dominated pets or cure those who have been infected by them earns their eternal ire. Yet it's succubi who enrage fungus tyrants the most. In these demons, the fungus tyrant sees a former glamour and power they no longer have access to, and only the brutal destruction of these mocking memories can salve a fungus tyrant's anger. A fungus tyrant faced with someone whom they suspect might be a succubus is terrible indeed, for in such battles, the fiendish fungal beings abandon their normal approach of subtle mental trickery and bring all of their power to bear in an attempt to rip the offending creature apart. Fungus tyrants also take great delight in transforming succubi into fungoid creatures under their control.
While their jealousy doesn't compel them to do battle with other fungus tyrants, they're always careful to maintain their own territories apart from any nearby kin, and they take great pains not to “poach” other tyrant's charmed and infested pets.
A fungus tyrant is 6 feet tall and weighs 150 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 31 **[Recall Knowledge - Fungus](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Fungus Tyrant](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3364) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 190 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); fungaltongue, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Fungaltongue** A fungus tyrant can speak with all forms of fungus, be they fungal creatures or otherwise. Most normal fungi don't recognize details about creatures or know anything about the world beyond their immediate vicinity. A fungus tyrant gains a +3 circumstance bonus on all Deception, Diplomacy, or Intimidation checks made against fungus creatures.
 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +17 **Will** +19 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 180 **Resistances** electricity 10 **Weaknesses** cold iron 10, good 10 --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** tentacle +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+7 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d12+7 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **3rd** [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Hallucinatory Terrain](/Spells.aspx?ID=145), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) - **5th** [Hallucination](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=193), [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Create Spawn** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) If a creature dies after being reduced to 0 HP by a fungus tyrant's Mind-Draining Kiss, the fungus tyrant can turn this victim into a spore thrall by donating some of their own fungal matter to the victim and leaving them to “ripen” within a patch of fungus for 24 hours. After this time, the creature is restored to life but gains the fungus trait and becomes a spore thrall. This prevents the creature's soul from continuing on to the afterlife in a similar manner to how undead are taken out of that cycle—as such, those who hold that cycle sacred (particularly worshipers of Pharasma) regard spore thralls as bad as the undead, despite them being living creatures. If the new spore thrall is a lower level than its creator, then it's under the creator's control. If a fungus tyrant controls too many spore thralls at once (as determined by the GM), strong-willed spore thralls can free themselves by succeeding at a DC 28 Will save. Typically, a fungus tyrant avoids creating spore thralls out of fallen foes who are too powerful to control, for they're only too aware of the compulsion for revenge such victims might harbor for those who wrenched them from the afterlife. **Grow Sporepod** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) The fungus tyrant causes a human-sized pod of fungal material to burst out of the ground at any point they can see within 60 feet. Once created, a sporepod lasts for 1 minute, until the fungus tyrant moves more than 120 feet away from it, or until it's destroyed (AC 28, Hardness 10, 20 HP). The fungus tyrant cannot Grow a Sporepod again for 1d4 rounds, but apart from this and a sporepod's 1 minute life, there is no hard limit to the number of sporepods a single fungus tyrant can have active at any one time. **Mind-Draining Kiss** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The fungus tyrant engages a creature they have grabbed in an act of passion in an attempt to drain its will and personality. The kiss makes the creature [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 or increases its stupefied condition by 1, to a maximum of 4. The creature takes 4d6 poison damage and must then succeed at a DC 28 Will save or be affected by a suggestion to remain immobile rather than trying to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296). **Sporestride** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) The fungus tyrant Steps into an adjacent sporepod and instantly teleports to any other active sporepod they've created, bursting out of the target sporepod and destroying it in the process. **Sporewarp** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) The fungus tyrant attempts a tentacle Strike but treats a sporepod of their choice within 120 feet that they can see as the point of origin of that tentacle Strike.
Spore Trall When a creature becomes a spore thrall, it rises from death with the same statistics it had in life, but gains the fungus trait, as well as immunity to death effects, disease, paralyzed, and poison, and its alignment switches to match that of the fungus tyrant who created them. When a spore thrall is slain, its soul finally travels to the Boneyard for judgment, but the fungus-encrusted physical remains often spawn new fungal creatures, such as [myceloids](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1242), [violet fungi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=853), or [drakauthixes](/Monsters.aspx?ID=158). These fungal spawn are not controlled by the fungus tyrant but generally regard them as an ally or, more often, a divinity.
Tanglebriar's Tyrants The festering reaches of Tanglebriar in southern Kyonin represent the region on Golarion where fungus tyrants are both the most common and most powerful. Many of Tanglebriar's fungus tyrants have potent druidic or sorcerous powers in addition to their innate divine spellcasting abilities and have developed unusual and violent variants of sporepods that give them even greater tactical options in battle.
name: Fungus Tyrant

The most widespread of the longlegs is the giant longlegs, a creature with a body the size of a dog whose long, spindly legs give it a much more intimidating appearance. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Giant Longlegs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3365) **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 192 **Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** -5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -4 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +10 **Will** +6 **HP** 50 **Cluster** A giant longlegs can share the same space as another giant longlegs, but no more than three giant longlegs can occupy the same space. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** jaws +11, **Damage** 1d10+6 piercing **Bob** The giant longlegs begins to bob up and down, using its legs almost like springs to cause its body to bounce and sway in an unsettling manner. Until the start of the giant longlegs's next turn, it gains a +2 status bonus to its Armor Class. **Noxious Spray** ([Olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) The giant longlegs sprays a noxious chemical onto a single adjacent target, who must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save. The giant longlegs can't use Noxious Spray for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune to Noxious Spray for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for as long as they remain sickened.
