markdown:
The Oliphaunt of Jandelay is drawn to worlds that are hurtling toward imminent annihilation. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 60 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 58 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 55 [Oliphaunt of Jandelay](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3407) **Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 195 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) 120 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +50 **Str** +12 **Dex** +5 **Con** +10 **Int** -1 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +48 **Ref** +37 **Will** +39 mythic resilience (all saves) **HP** 680 , regeneration 30 **Immunities** [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), mythic immunity, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93), [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 20, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 20, physical 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 300 feet, DC 46
**Mythic Immunity** The Oliphaunt of Jandelay is immune to harmful spells cast by non-mythic creatures, Strikes made with non-mythic weapons, and unarmed Strikes from nonmythic characters.
**Mythic Resilience** The Oliphaunt treats its saving throws with the associated save as one step better than they actually are (so a critical failure is a failure, a failure is a success, and a success is a critical success). The first time each round that a character rolls a critical hit against the Oliphaunt, they must reroll the attack roll and take the new result.
**Reactive** The Oliphaunt of Jandelay gains 3 reactions each round. It can still use only one reaction per trigger.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 40 feet; legendary vigor, [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Melee** tusk +45 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+22 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Melee** foot +45 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+22 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Melee** trunk +45 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+22 bludgeoning **Ranged** debris toss +43 ([deadly 2d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 150 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+16 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 46 - **10th** [Cataclysm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1460) - **Constant (4th)** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) **Mythic Power** 3 Mythic Points
  • _Remove a Condition_ ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** The Oliphaunt of Jandelay removes any one condition currently affecting it.
  • _Undying Myth_ **Cost** all the creature's Mythic Points; **Effect** When the Oliphaunt of Jandelay would die, as long as it has at least 1 Mythic Point, it immediately expends all remaining Mythic Points it has, remains standing and conscious, and recovers 50% of its maximum Hit Points.
**Brutal Drag** When the Oliphaunt takes a Stride action, any creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by the Oliphaunt of Jandelay's tusk is moved along with it to a location within 20 feet of the Oliphaunt's ending location. They remain grabbed and the GM determines which square they end up in. **Destructive Frenzy** The Oliphaunt of Jandelay makes two tusk Strikes, two foot Strikes, and one trunk Strike in any order. **Devastating Launch** **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** The Oliphaunt of Jandelay buries its tusks into the ground in a 15- foot burst centered within 20 feet of it, collecting everything in that area, including any creature currently [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) by its tusk attack, and flinging it all up to 120 feet away. Everything lands in a 30-foot burst, dealing 12d6 bludgeoning damage to each creature in the area (DC 51 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). This damage also applies to any creatures, vehicles, or objects the Oliphaunt flung as well. A creature in the area being flung that succeeds or critically succeeds on their Reflex save remains in the square they started in. Anything launched by the Oliphaunt lands in a square in the burst as determined by the GM. **Legendary Vigor** The Oliphaunt of Jandelay is immune to penalties to its Speeds, and it ignores [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and greater difficult terrain. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Huge or smaller, foot, DC 46. When the Oliphaunt of Jandelay Tramples, it can Stride up to triple its Speed, and any creature that fails or critically fails the Reflex save is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Trumpeting Blast** ([Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) The Oliphaunt of Jandelay emits a cacophonous blast from its trunk in a 120-foot cone. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 49 Fortitude save. If the Oliphaunt spends 1 Mythic Point as part of the activity, the DC becomes 51 and the damage dice are increased to d12s. The Oliphaunt of Jandelay can't use Trumpeting Blast again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 7d10 sonic damage and is [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature takes 14d10 sonic damage, is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) for 2 rounds, and is deafened for 2 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 28d10 sonic damage, is slowed 2 for 2 rounds, and is deafened for 4 rounds.
name: Oliphaunt of Jandelay

A sublime breath seeks to perfect their craft, or, when they see the seed of potential in a worthy mortal, to inspire them to new heights. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Sublime Breath](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3408) **Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 201 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +26, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +15, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +26 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +5 **Immaculate Instrument** A sublime breath carries a single tool, prop, or instrument related to its chosen craft, such as a mask, sash, or paintbrush. As long as they possess their immaculate instrument, they treat any critical failures on [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks as failures.
**Artistic Specialist** In a recital, competition, or other measure of artistic skill, a sublime breath is a 12th-level challenge.
**Thought Slips Away** The sublime breath's ephemeral lightness makes them impossible to grasp. They use their [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) modifier to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378). When they [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), High Jump, or Long Jump, the movement does not provoke reactions. **Items** [_immaculate instrument_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3514) --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +14 **Will** +17 mythic resilience (Will) **HP** 111 **Mythic Resilience** The sublime breath treats their Will saving throws as one step better than it actually is (so a critical failure is a failure, a failure is a success, and a success is a critical success). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** soft touch +16 ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)), **Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning plus 1d6 mental and 1d6 spirit **Ranged** feigned strike +16 ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)), **Damage** 2d6 mental and 2d6 spirit **Mythic Power** 3 Mythic Points
  • _Remove a Condition_ ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** The sublime breath removes any one condition currently affecting them.
**Artistic Creation** ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The sublime breath's art is so real that it takes shape in the hearts of those who witness it. The sublime breath crafts, acts, recites a tale or song, or otherwise uses their art to create a work of art in a 10-foot burst within 60 feet. The creation is perceptible and tangible to creatures that don't disbelieve it and it affects them accordingly; for instance a ladder could be climbed, and a campfire would provide heat and even be able to cook food for an affected creature. A hazardous creation deals 4d6 damage to creatures that enter or begin their turn in the area (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save), of a type matching the creation, usually a physical damage type or a common energy type such as fire, acid, or cold. The creation lasts until the end of the sublime breath's next turn, though the sublime breath can Sustain it to prolong the effect for up to 1 minute. The sublime breath can Sustain any number of Artistic Creations with a single action. **Artistic Destruction** ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** The sublime breath has either used Artistic Creation this turn or Sustained an Artistic Creation this turn; **Effect** The sublime breath destroys their creations to make way for new growth. All of their currently sustained Artistic Creations detonate, dealing 8d6 damage to all enemy creatures either within a creation or within a 10-foot burst of it (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save). The damage type matches the creation. Creatures in multiple overlapping bursts take damage only once, of the type of their choice. The sublime breath then cannot use Artistic Creation for 1d4 turns. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The sublime breath can take on the appearance of any Medium or Large [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) creature. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes, but might change the damage type their Strikes deal.
The sublime breath instinctively takes on the appearance an observer finds most inspiring. The first time they become [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84), they use Change Shape as a free action, even if they were unaware they were being observed, they take on a specific appearance reflecting the hidden desires, hopes, artistic inclinations, or similar deep-seated emotions of a single observer. As long as the sublime breath can be observed by this creature and maintains this shape, the observer gains a +1 circumstance bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks and takes a –1 circumstance penalty to Will saves against the sublime breath or to any check or DC that would attempt to capture or restrain them, such as attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) or the DC of a [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) spell. **Hours Go By** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A sublime breath's presence can allow artists and artisans to work almost effortlessly in a state of perfect flow. The sublime breath encourages a single creature within 60 feet, who becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89). They can spend the extra action only to Sustain a spell or other ability.
name: Sublime Breath

Once an [orc](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=454) god, Verex-That-Was has been reshaped into a towering creature of mangled flesh and bone. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 58 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 56 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 53 [Verex-That-Was](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3409) **Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 207 **Perception** +42; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Orcish](/Languages.aspx?ID=9) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +45, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +45 **Str** +12 **Dex** +9 **Con** +11 **Int** +7 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 51 **Fort** +42 **Ref** +38 **Will** +36 mythic resilience (all saves) **HP** 550 , regeneration 30 (deactivated by sloughstone weapons) **Immunities** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), mythic immunity, [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 25, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 25, physical 20 (except [sloughstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3515) and holy) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 20 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 150 feet, DC 45
**Mythic Immunity** Verex-That-Was is immune to harmful spells cast by non-mythic creatures, Strikes made with non-mythic weapons, and unarmed Strikes from non-mythic characters.
**Mythic Resilience** Verex-That-Was treats his saving throws with the associated save as one step better than they actually are (so a critical failure is a failure, a failure is a success, and a success is a critical success).
**Symphony of Pain** **Trigger** A creature within Verex-That-Was's frightful presence damages Verex-That-Was; **Effect** Verex unleashes an echo of psychic pain throughout his vicinity, inflicting mental damage equal to half of the damage he sustained on all creatures, friend or foe, within 150 feet (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). --- **Speed** 50 feet, burrow 30 feet **Melee** claw +44 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 5d10+22 slashing plus bloodboils **Melee** jaws +44 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+22 piercing plus bloodboils and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Melee** tail +44 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+22 bludgeoning plus bloodboils **Ranged** teeth +42 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 5d6+16 piercing **Mythic Power** 3 Mythic Points
  • **Undying Myth** **Cost** all Verex's Mythic Points; **Trigger** Verex-That-Was would die and has at least 1 Mythic Point; Effect Verex remains standing and conscious, and recovers 50% of his maximum Hit Points.
**Battlefield Eruption** ([Mythic](/Traits.aspx?ID=835), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** Verex-That-Was burrows into the ground, reemerging anywhere on the same plane where either he or his worshippers have previously killed at least 10 sapient creatures in battle. A fountain of gore and shattered bone erupts from the ground when Verex arrives, dealing 16d10 piercing damage in a 30-foot emanation (DC 48 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). **Bloodboils** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578)) Wounds caused by direct contact with Verex-That-Was erupt into painful, blood-filled boils. A creature struck by Verex-That-Was's jaws or tail must succeed at a DC 45 Fortitude or gain weakness 10 to physical damage for 1 day as the boils explode messily each time the creature sustains another wound. The bloodboils can be counteracted with a 3rd-rank (or higher) [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) spell, but the disease also ends if the victim receives magical healing that brings them to their Hit Point maximum. **Leap into the Fray** Verex-That-Was [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) up to 20 feet horizontally and up to 10 feet vertically and then makes one claw Strike, one jaws Strike, and one tail Strike in any order. If Verex-That-Was spends 1 Mythic Point as part of this action, he doubles the distances he can Leap and each creature he lands adjacent to is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of his turn. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** Huge, 4d10+10 bludgeoning, Rupture 50 **War Cry of Destruction** ([Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) Verex-That-Was unleashes a terrible howl in a 60-foot-cone, dealing 12d6 sonic damage and 12d6 void damage to all creatures (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). The damage bypasses up to 25 Hardness of materials other than [sloughstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3515). Creatures in the area with the [orc](/Traits.aspx?ID=666) trait can use a reaction to roar in defiance of their corrupted god, granting themselves a +2 circumstance bonus on the saving throw but extending the area of the war cry to also include a 30-foot emanation centered on themself; orc creatures also automatically howl in their death throes if they are reduced to 0 Hit Points by this ability. This area can extend as long as orc creatures remain within the area and choose to roar, but creatures within multiple overlapping areas of effect must attempt only one save. Verex-That-Was cannot loose another War Cry of Destruction for 1d4 rounds.
name: Verex-That-Was

Once a servitor of an ancient god of secrets, the Weaver of Webs now seeks to attain her own godhood. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Weaver Of Webs](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3410) **Source** [War of Immortals](/Sources.aspx?ID=232) pg. 214 **Perception** +32; [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49), [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) ([imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=412)) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118); [truespeech](/Feats.aspx?ID=2296) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +30, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +30, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +30, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +25, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +27, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +27, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +30 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +8 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +6 **Countless Eyes** ([scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689)) The Weaver of Webs can see through the eyes of any spider, living or dead, in one of her many lairs. When the Weaver casts her [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) spell and targets a spider in her lair, the spell is not expended, and the spider automatically critically fails its saving throw.
**Greater Web Sense** The Weaver of Webs' tremorsense also extends to any of her webs, regardless of distance or area, and her tremorsense is a [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=411) sense against any creature in contact with one of her webs. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +23 **Will** +29 **HP** 335 , regeneration 10 (deactivated by [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407)) **Immunities** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10, mythic resistance 15, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 10 **Mythic Resistance** The Weaver of Webs has resistance 15 to all attacks made with non-[mythic](/Traits.aspx?ID=835) weapons and unarmed attacks made by non-mythic creatures.
**Spilled Secrets** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet. Any creature that speaks within this aura must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or divulge some kind of secret instead of whatever speech they intended. [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) spells and effects gain the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait if they didn't have it already and are wasted if the creature fails this save. On a critical failure, the character betrays a secret they least want to reveal to the Weaver or those present.
**Adopted Brood** **Trigger** A creature deals precision damage to the Weaver; **Effect** A spray of smaller spiders pours out of the open wound. These spiders deal 3d6 piercing damage to a single creature within 15 feet of the Weaver and expose that creature to Weaver venom before skittering away. --- **Speed** 60 feet, climb 60 feet **Melee** fangs +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+16 piercing plus Weaver venom **Melee** tarsal claw +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+16 slashing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Ranged** web +24 ([Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d4+10 bludgeoning plus nightmare cocoon and Weaver venom **Occult Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +28 - **2nd** [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) (at will) - **3rd** [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=2045) (at will) - **5th** [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665) (at will) - **6th** [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630), [Repulsion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1650), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662), [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) - **7th** [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) - **8th** [Dream Council](/Spells.aspx?ID=1988) - **Constant (7th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Mythic Power** 3 Mythic Points
  • _Remove a Condition_ ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** The Weaver removes any one condition currently affecting her.
**Nightmare Cocoon** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A creature struck by the Weaver's web Strike must succeed at a DC 34 Reflex save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 34). If the Weaver spends 1 Mythic Point as a free action when a creature fails this save, the creature is also [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), experiencing terrible nightmares of their deepest-held anxieties. The Weaver can view these dreams using a Sustain action, which imparts a –2 circumstance penalty to any Will saves the target attempts against the Weaver's spells until the beginning of the Weaver's next turn. At the end of a paralyzed victim's turn, they can attempt a DC 34 Will save to end the paralyzed condition (though they are still immobilized until they Escape). **Weaver Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 34; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d10 poison (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d10 poison and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) (2 rounds); **Stage 3** 3d10 poison and slowed 2 (1 round); **Stage 4** 4d10 poison (1 round) and the target permanently forgets the Weaver exists, including any previous mention of her. **Webbed Conveyance** **Requirements** The Weaver is within 15 feet of a creature [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) in her nightmare cocoon; **Effect** The Weaver grabs the paralyzed target, webs them to her back or one of her legs, and then Strides. As long as the creature is immobilized by the Weaver's nightmare cocoon, it shares the Weaver's space and moves with her. The DC to Escape the nightmare cocoon increases to 36.
name: Weaver Of Webs

<title level="1" pfs="">[Apprentice](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3411)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Ambitious apprentices can be found in all cities. These individuals are generally younger and seek the approval of their masters as they learn their craft. Many yearn to exemplify the artistry behind their craft, one day becoming masters themselves. Depicted below is an apprentice cartographer.

Small settlements can typically support one or two generalist merchants, and larger cities house multiple specialists—experts in one type of product. Merchants range from vendors hustling in the public square to wealthy tycoons running entire commercial organizations. A merchant might have an additional [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill about a specific category of item (such as jewelry or magic weapons), with a skill modifier equal to their Mercantile Lore. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Merchant](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3412) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 8 **Perception** +6 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Mercantile Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +8, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +8 **Str** +2 **Dex** +0 **Con** -1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Sales Specialist** For encounters involving negotiation or mercantile skill, a merchant is a 4th-level challenge.
**Appraising Eye** The merchant can use [Mercantile Lore](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=362) to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about items, including determining their value. They can also attempt to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365) using Mercantile Lore and can do so without first knowing whether the item is [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644). **Items** [Club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=2), [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=3) --- **AC** 13 **Fort** +1 **Ref** +2 **Will** +10 **HP** 7 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** club +4, **Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +4 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning **Ranged** club +4 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+2 bludgeoning

<title level="1" pfs="">[Guildmaster](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3414)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

In cities, artisans working in a common trade often form guilds to set standards of quality, establish common prices, engage in collective bargaining with business owners, and lobby local governments for favorable laws. The guildmaster— often a master artisan in their own right—also acts as an administrator and politician, advocating for artisans in their trade.

<column gap="tiny">

([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

Amid the political machinations of the court stand the court historians, observing and recording events for future generations. Their loyalty is to the preservation of history, and they will defy even royalty in the name of truth. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Court Historian](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3415) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 12 **Perception** +7 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Genealogy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Scribing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +9 **Str** +0 **Dex** +1 **Con** -1 **Int** +5 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Historical Specialist** In matters regarding history or court records, the court historian is a 5th-level challenge.
**Records Don't Lie** The court historian has a Perception DC of 25 against [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks asserting false current or historical events. **Items** court record, [fine clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715), inkwell, [Writing Set](/Equipment.aspx?ID=63) --- **AC** 13 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +5 **Will** +9 **HP** 7 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **Ranged** inkwell +5 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **Distracting Diatribe** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The court historian monotonously recites facts to distract a creature within 30 feet that can hear them. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
name: Court Historian

markdown:
Envoys are guests of a given court, representing the interests of another court or organization. Some envoys stay in one place so long they're practically considered locals, though those with whom they parley are swiftly reminded where their loyalty lies. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Envoy](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3416) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 12 **Perception** +7; (13 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); plus two additional languages **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +15 **Str** +0 **Dex** +1 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 **Diplomatic Specialist** When dealing with matters of statecraft and negotiation, the envoy is a 6th-level challenge. **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [fine clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715), letter of diplomatic status, signet ring --- **AC** 13 **Fort** +2 **Ref** +3 **Will** +11 **HP** 12 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dagger +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+2 piercing **Melee** fist +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning **Ranged** dagger +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+2 piercing **Diplomatic Immunity** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The envoy invokes their diplomatic status. Until the end of the envoy's next turn, any creature that attempts to attack them must succeed at a DC 15 Will save or have their attack [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342). The attacker gains weakness 2 to all damage from the envoy's allies while Diplomatic Immunity lasts, whether their attack was disrupted or not.
The envoy can Sustain this ability. If the envoy takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300), Diplomatic Immunity ends and can't be used again for 1 hour.
name: Envoy

markdown:
Courtesans are high-class entertainers and professional companions, paid in favors and privileges for the honor of their company. These trendsetting socialites levy their clout to consort with those above their station, maneuvering connections to build exclusive clientele and climb the social ladder. A courtesan's discretion and mobility among the different ranks of society make their friendship well worth the price. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Courtesan](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3417) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 13 **Perception** +9; (13 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); plus two additional languages **Skills** [Art Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +10 **Str** -1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Group Impression** When the courtesan [Makes an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), they can compare their [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check result to the Will DCs of up to four targets instead of one.
**Social Specialist** When entertaining or socializing, the courtesan is a 5th-level challenge. **Items** [fine clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715), [flute](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737), jewelry, [sword cane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2772) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +7 **Will** +11 **HP** 25 **Beguiling Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 10 feet. Creatures in the area that can observe the courtesan take a –2 status penalty on their Will DC against the courtesan's attempts to make a [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) of them. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** sword cane +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Concealable](/Traits.aspx?ID=560), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing **Melee** fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Cutting Remarks** The courtesan levies insults or backhanded compliments, attempting to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) a creature using their [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) modifier instead of [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40). **Words of Encouragement** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The courtesan praises the performance of one ally who can hear them. The targeted ally ignores any circumstance and status penalties they have until the start of the courtesan's next turn. The target then becomes temporarily immune to this ability for 10 minutes.
Elegant Establishments Courtesans thrive both in business and status by catering to the elite upper class through creating private, exclusive spaces such as theaters, art galleries, tea houses, and boutiques. This allows both the courtesans and the upper class to strengthen alliances and accumulate secrets.
name: Courtesan

<title level="1" pfs="">[Noble](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3418)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Envied by many and detested by some, nobles exude confidence and gentility. Gossip and gambling are often nobles' favorite pastimes. Day-to-day life for a noble is usually a mixture of business and leisure, and to an onlooker, such a lifestyle can appear to be nothing more than a string of meals, parties, and game halls.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Noble](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3418)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 13

**Perception** +11

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +10, [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +9, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Lip Reader** After years of sticking their nose where it doesn't belong, the noble has learned to read lips from afar. If they're trying to read lips in an encounter or attempting a difficult feat of lip reading, they are [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), and might need to succeed at a [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) check with a DC determined by the GM.

**Items**
[fashionable fine clothes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715), [Loaded Dice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3582), [Rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=36), [silver flask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=277), signet ring

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +10 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 50

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rapier +10 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning

**Noble's Ruse** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The noble [FeintS](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390). On a success, the noble Strikes the target.

**Sneak Attack**   The noble deals 1d6 extra precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

Often the younger offspring of minor nobility or those from long lines of trusted staff, palace guards are in charge of defending the royal family and their inner stronghold. Their days consist of guarding doorways, escorting nobles, and keeping those in their charge as safe as possible. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Palace Guard](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3419) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 14 **Perception** +12; (14 when rolling initiative) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +8 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=46) with palace insignia, [Halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=26), [simple manacles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=32) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +10 **Will** +10 **HP** 60 **Guard's Parry **Trigger** A creature attacks the palace guard's liege, and the liege is within the guard's melee reach; **Effect** The liege gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack, and the palace guard gains a +2 circumstance bonus to attack and damage rolls until the end of their next turn.
**Reactive Strike --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** halberd +14 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d10+7 piercing **Melee** fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning **Crowd Control** **Requirements** The palace guard's last action was a successful halberd Strike; **Effect** The palace guard attempts to [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379) the creature they hit using their halberd's reach. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward the guard's multiple attack penalty
name: Palace Guard

<title level="1" pfs="">[Advisor](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3420)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Trusted counselors of the court, advisors whisper words of guidance into the ears of those in power. Many nobles lean so heavily on their advisor's counsel that they make few decisions without them and insist on their attendance at all meetings and public events. Advisors are often master manipulators.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

**Placate** An advisor is well versed in soothing agitated nobles. Their calming voice gives them a +2 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks when dealing with members of the nobility.

**Items**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (2), [fine clothes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715), _[minor healing potion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2943)_, [small harp](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)

<traits>

markdown:
Veteran nobles have survived battles in the past, both social and physical. These movers and shakers are often the patrons or mentors of bold adventurers. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Veteran Noble](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3422) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 15 **Perception** +15 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Heraldry Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14 **Str** +3 **Dex** +2 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12), _+1 [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_, signet ring --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +14 **Will** +16 **HP** 85 **Battle Scarred** The first time each day the veteran noble would be reduced to 0 HP, they remain at 1 HP and are [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) for the rest of the day.
**Noble Pride** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** An opponent attempts to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) the veteran noble or one of the noble's allies within 30 feet; **Effect** The veteran noble attempts to Demoralize the triggering opponent before the opponent rolls. On a critical success, the triggering action is [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342) as well. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d8+11 slashing **Melee** dagger +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+11 piercing **Melee** gauntlet +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Free-Hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613)), **Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** dagger +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+11 piercing **Tactical Command** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The veteran noble directs an ally. The ally can immediately use their reaction to Strike or to Stride without triggering reactions. The ally gains a +2 status bonus to their Strike if the veteran noble has dealt with that creature or an organization that creature belongs to before, as the veteran offers hard-earned tactical advice.
name: Veteran Noble

markdown:
Elite fighters from the lowest ranks of nobility, knights are proud champions of their court. Unlike other nobles, knights must earn their title through loyalty and strength-of-arms rather than inheritance. Ideals such as chivalry, honor, and virtue are associated with knights but not all meet such romantic standards. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Knight](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3423) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 16 **Perception** +13 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +13, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Items** _+1 [bastard sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=370)_, [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50) with livery, [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=10) (3), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, 20 HP, BT 10) --- **AC** 25 (27 with shield raised) **Fort** +14 **Ref** +14 **Will** +13 **HP** 110 **Knight's Courage** Any time the knight gains the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition, they reduce its value by 1.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)** The knight can Shield Block for an adjacent ally, preventing that ally from taking damage instead of themself. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _bastard sword_ +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d8+10 slashing **Melee** spear +17, **Damage** 1d6+10 piercing **Melee** gauntlet +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Free-Hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** spear +17 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+10 piercing **Intimidating Strike** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [Fighter](/Traits.aspx?ID=601), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The knight makes a melee Strike. If it hits and deals damage, the target is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), or frightened 2 on a critical hit. **Rearming Advance** The knight Strides or Steps. During this movement, they can Interact to swap from wielding their bastard sword in two hands to wielding it in one hand and wielding their shield in the other, or vice versa. This Interact action doesn't trigger reactions that can be triggered by [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) actions. **Warding Shift** **Requirements** The knight is adjacent to a willing ally; **Effect** The knight moves an adjacent willing ally 5 feet in any direction and can Step into the space the ally vacated.
Knighthood While most are knighted after years of training under an established knight, there are other ways to earn the title. It might be presented as a tournament prize, a reward for prowess in battle, or an honor bestowed upon adventurers for aiding a grateful noble. Knighthood is the fastest way to join the ranks of nobility outside of marriage and bestows land and properties along with a rise in station.
name: Knight

<title level="1" pfs="">[Grave Robber](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3424)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Many cultures have a tradition of burying their dead with a selection of the deceased's most precious possessions. Such valuables can be easy pickings for those with no respect for—or fear of—the dead.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Grave_Robber.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 18

**Perception** +5

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +6, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +2, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +2, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +5, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +2

**Con** +2

**Int** +3

**Wis** +2

</row>

embalming flask, [Holy Water](/Equipment.aspx?ID=245), shovel

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +7 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +5 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shovel +6,
**Damage** 1d6+1 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
embalming flask +7 ([Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699)),
**Damage** 1d4 acid plus 1 acid splash damage and alchemical embalming

**Alchemical Embalming**   The grave robber carries alchemical vials of specially prepared embalming fluid meant to hinder pursuit by anyone who interrupts their grave-robbing. A creature hit by a grave robber's embalming flask takes a –10-foot penalty to all its Speeds for 1 round. On a critical hit from an embalming flask, the target is also [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) for 1 minute.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Bandit](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3425)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Bandits waylay travelers and plunder their valuables before disappearing back to their wilderness hideouts. Many bandits seek only to steal and release their victims alive, though a few prefer to leave no witnesses.

**Bandit's Ambush** When the bandit rolls initiative using [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48), they can attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) one creature as a free action.

**Items**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [Machete](/Weapons.aspx?ID=95), [Sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) (10 bullets), [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+5 piercing

Loan sharks lend money to those in need but charge high interest. If there's ever an issue with repayment, they'll send their gang to ensure clients pay in full. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Loan Shark](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3426) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 19 **Perception** +8 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Accounting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +8, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +8, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +15 **Str** +3 **Dex** +0 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 **Business Savvy** When making monetary deals, the loan shark gets a +8 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks, and their Perception DC.
**Loan Specialist** For encounters involving monetary deals, the loan shark is a 7th-level challenge. **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), dragon-headed cane (functions as a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +6 **Will** +10 **HP** 25 **Never off the Hook** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet. Creatures in the aura who owe the loan shark money take a –3 circumstance penalty to their Will DC against the loan shark's attempts to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) or [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) them and can't reduce their frightened value below 1 while in the aura. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dragon-headed cane +9 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning **Interest is Due!** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The loan shark commands an ally within 30 feet to attack a creature who owes the loan shark money. The ally can use a reaction to Strike the debtor, dealing an additional 1d6 mental damage.
name: Loan Shark

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
club +9,
**Damage** 1d6+5 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning

**Ranged**

club +8 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)),

**Brutal Beating**   The ruffian's brutality shakes foes' confidence. When the ruffian deals damage on a critical hit, the target is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), and the ruffian can [push](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) the target up to 10 feet.

**Combat Grab** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Trigger** The ruffian has one hand free; **Effect** The ruffian makes a melee Strike while keeping one hand free. If this Strike hits, the ruffian [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target using their free hand. The creature remains [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) until the end of the ruffian's next turn or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296), whichever comes first.

**Sneak Attack**   The ruffian deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Charlatan](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3428)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Charlatans use pretense and misdirection to swindle money and other valuables from the credulous and confused.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 3">[Charlatan](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3428)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 20

**Perception** +6

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +9, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +7, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +11, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +8, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +9

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +2

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Versatile Performance** The charlatan can use their [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) skill to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395), and they can use an acting Performance to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388).

**Items**
[Disguise Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), lute, [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43), [Thieves' Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758), [Whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=48)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 40

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
whip +10 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+2 bludgeoning

**Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 20, attack +12
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)
- **1st**
[Disguise Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1491), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (3 slots)
- **2nd**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (2 slots)

**Bard Composition Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 19
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Courageous Anthem](/Spells.aspx?ID=1763)
- **1st**
[Counter Performance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1762)

**Sneak Attack**   The charlatan deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-533" />
name: Charlatan

These criminals specialize in breaking and entering, gaining access to secure buildings and bypassing security measures undetected. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Burglar](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3429) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 20 **Perception** +10; (11 to find traps) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +12, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +7 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Climbing Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=14), [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (10 arrows), [lesser darkvision elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=89), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43), [Thieves' Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) --- **AC** 21 (22 vs. traps) **Fort** +7 **Ref** +12 **Will** +10 +1 circumstance to all saves vs. traps **HP** 60 **Deny Advantage** The burglar isn't [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to creatures of 4th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), flanking, or using surprise attack.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The burglar is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker it can see; **Effect** The burglar gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** shortsword +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing **Melee** fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning **Ranged** composite shortbow +14 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing **Mobility** When the burglar Strides half their Speed or less, that movement does not trigger reactions. **Sneak Attack** The burglar deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **Surprise Attack** On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the Burglar.
name: Burglar

<title level="1" pfs="">[Fence](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3430)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Fences make themselves indispensable to the underworld by paying for stolen goods only to resell them later, whether through a seemingly legitimate business or to a closed group of elite buyers.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Fence](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3430)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 21

**Perception** +11

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Accounting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +10, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +4

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Fence's Eye** Fences can use [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) to identify an item's value and [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365) on an item. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Underworld Lore checks when doing so, and to all Underworld Lore checks related to stolen items.

**Items**
[Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (10), [lesser darkvision elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=89), [Disguise Kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43), [lesser smoke ball](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3360) (2), [Thieves' Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 70

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
shortsword +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+6 piercing

**Fence's Feint** <actions string="Single Action" />  The fence [Feints](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390), then can Step. If the Feint succeeds, the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the fence's melee attacks until the end of the fence's next turn (or to all melee attacks on a critical success).

**Quick Rummage** <actions string="Single Action" />  The fence always has a few items close at hand. The fence Interacts to draw a weapon or an item that takes a single action to activate, and then Strikes with the weapon or Activates the Item.

**Sneak Attack**   The fence deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-533" />
name: Fence

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Criminal organizations are always happy to loan out money at exorbitant rates, and their legbreakers are always happy to collect.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Legbreaker](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3431)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 21

**Perception** +14

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +3

**Con** +3

**Int** -1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [maul](/Weapons.aspx?ID=388)_, [studded leather](/Armor.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110

</row>

**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** <actions string="Reaction" /> 

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_maul_ +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)),
**Damage** 1d10+10 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning

**Break Legs!** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The legbreaker makes a maul Strike against an adjacent creature. If it hits, the creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) for 1 minute. As long as this clumsy condition lasts, the creature also takes a –5-foot penalty to its Speeds and has weakness 5 to the legbreaker's Strikes.

**Rushing Strike** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The legbreaker Strides twice. If they end their movement within melee reach of an enemy, they can make a melee Strike against that enemy.

**Stampeding Shove** <actions string="Single Action" />  The legbreaker [Shoves](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) a creature, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to their Athletics check if the target is [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). If the Shove succeeds, the target takes 2d10 bludgeoning damage (double damage on a critical success).

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-533" />
name: Legbreaker

markdown:
Cutthroat crews of criminals form gangs to take out larger scores and intimidate their victims. Bandit gangs are often accompanied by a gang leader. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Bandit Gang](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3432) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 22 **Perception** +15 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +11, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +16 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Lie in Wait** ([exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595)) The troop can spend 10 minutes preparing the ground before combat to gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their initiative roll.
**Sudden Ambush** When the troop rolls initiative using [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48), they can use Stand and Deliver! as a free action. --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +16 **Will** +13 **HP** 120 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 8, splash damage 8 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; forest passage, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Chop ‘em Down!** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The bandits make a coordinated attack with their axes against each enemy in a 5-foot emanation with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d6+3 slashing damage
2d6+9 slashing damage
3d6+9 slashing damage **Forest Passage** The bandit ignores any [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) caused by plants, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth. **Launch Slings!** The bandits draw or reload their slings, then launch a volley of sling bullets. This is a 10-foot burst within 50 feet that deals 2d6+4 bludgeoning damage with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. When the troop is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Stand and Deliver!** The troop attempts to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) up to 4 creatures.
name: Bandit Gang

markdown:
A master of disguise uses costuming, makeup, and minor illusions to deceive. Some conceal their identity for years, infiltrating organizations under deep cover. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Master Of Disguise](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3433) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 22 **Perception** +17; (21 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=3), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Halfling](/Languages.aspx?ID=7) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +16, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +16, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +15, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Deep Cover** At most times, a master of disguise has infiltrated a specific organization, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391), [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388), [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389), or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) when dealing with its members.
**Disguise Specialist** For social encounters involving impersonation, the master of disguise is a 10th-level challenge. **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) (5), [elite disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), [_masquerade scarf_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3095) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +17 **Will** +16 **HP** 110 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dagger +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+6 piercing **Melee** fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** dagger +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+6 piercing **Double Take** If the master of disguise and the creature they're [Impersonating](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) are in each others' presence, the genuine creature must [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) if they're vouching for their own identity, and are treated as though they were Impersonating themself if someone [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) in an attempt to pierce their disguise. The genuine creature can use their [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) modifier, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) modifier, or a +15 modifier, whichever is highest. **Impeccable Disguise** The master of disguise creates a disguise and [Impersonates](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388). They gain a +5 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to Impersonate or to tell a [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) that helps them maintain their disguise. When a spell or magical effect tries to read their mind, detect whether they're lying, or reveal their identity, they can attempt a Deception check against the spell or effect's DC. If they succeed, the effect reveals information appropriate to their cover identity or nothing (the GM determines which). **Shocking Reveal** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The master of disguise removes their disguise with a dramatic gesture. Any creatures that previously failed to see through the disguise is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the master of disguise until the end of the turn. **Sneak Attack** The master of disguise deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.
Know Your Enemy A master of disguise presents an opportunity for GMs to make past events relevant again. An NPC who was previously significant may well have been a master of disguise who is now revealing themself to the PCs years later, opening a web of connections the PCs never knew existed until now.
name: Master Of Disguise

Assassins commit murder, either for pay or due to their belief in a cause, such as a religion or a political movement. Many are members of assassins' guilds: organizations that accept contracts to kill in return for money, favors, or both. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Assassin](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3434) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 23 **Perception** +16 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +10, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +15, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435)_ (20 arrows), [lesser darkvision elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=89), [Giant Centipede Venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3334) (4), [_invisibility potion_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2944), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Lethargy Poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=120) (3), _+1 [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=391)_ --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +19 **Will** +14 **HP** 130 **Deny Advantage** The assassin isn't [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to creatures of 8th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), flanking, or using surprise attack.
**Nimble Dodge** **Trigger** The assassin is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker it can see; **Effect** The assassin gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swift sneak **Melee** _rapier_ +20 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 1d6+9 piercing **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+9 bludgeoning **Ranged** _composite shortbow_ +20 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d6+7 piercing **Assassin's Poison** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The assassin is wielding a piercing or slashing weapon and has a free hand; **Effect** The assassin applies a poison to the weapon. That poison's DC is increased to 24 if it was lower. **Quick Draw** The assassin Interacts to draw a weapon, then Strikes with that weapon. **Sneak Attack** The assassin deals an extra 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures. **Surprise Attack** On the first round of combat, creatures that haven't acted yet are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the assassin. **Swift Sneak** The assassin can move their full Speed when [Sneaking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405).
name: Assassin
