markdown:
Kingpins build empires from the shadows, ruling their territory ruthlessly and keeping their business private. Most forge connections with the rich and powerful, doing dirty work for politicians and minor nobles in return for influence and favors that can be called in at any time. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Crime Kingpin](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3435) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 24 **Perception** +22; (24 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +24, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +24, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [hand crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=427)_ (10 bolts), _+1 [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_, [_potion of flying_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2948), [_moderate potion of healing_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2943) (2), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [rapier](/Weapons.aspx?ID=391)_ --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +23 **Will** +22 **HP** 250 **Kingpin's Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet. Allies in the aura gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects.
**Deny Advantage** The kingpin isn't [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to creatures of 12th level or lower that are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), flanking, or using surprise attack.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**You'll Pay for That** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** An enemy damages the kingpin; **Effect** The kingpin issues a vendetta against the enemy. Each of the kingpin's allies who hears the command gains a +5 status bonus to their next damage roll against that enemy. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _rapier_ +26 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+11 piercing **Melee** fist +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning **Ranged** _hand crossbow_ +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d6+8 piercing **Fencing Brawl** The kingpin attempts a rapier Strike followed by a [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) or [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) attempt against the same enemy. These count as one attack for the kingpin's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks. **Kick Away** **Trigger** The kingpin knocks an item out of a creature's grasp using [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383); **Effect** The kingpin kicks the weapon up to 20 feet in any direction. If the kingpin kicks the weapon into an ally's square, that ally can catch the weapon as a free action, Releasing anything else they're holding if necessary. **Kingpin's Command** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The crime kingpin shouts a command to an ally of their choice. That ally can spend a reaction to Stride and Strike. The ally becomes immune to Kingpin's Command for 24 hours. **Sneak Attack** The crime kingpin deals an additional 3d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.
name: Crime Kingpin

markdown:
There's no honor among thieves, but if there were points for style, these thieves would have 10s across the board. Some would say flamboyant thieves value showcasing their skills rather than successfully stealing an item, but what better way to show that you're the best than with an audience? **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 39 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 [Flamboyant Thief](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3436) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 25 **Perception** +27 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +26, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +28, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +31, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +31, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +28 **Str** +5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Flamboyant Performance** A flamboyant thief's attempts to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) don't automatically fail even if a creature is in combat or on guard. While being [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84), the thief gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=2390) and to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks to [Palm an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=2409) or Steal. However, they are compelled to leave a tangible sign of their presence, such as a calling card or symbol—often in place of a stolen item.
**Vanishing Act** The flamboyant thief can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) even without having [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2845) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_, [elite disguise kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=19), [fine clothes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1773), _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_, _[potion of flying](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2948)_, [greater smoke ball](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3360), [infiltrator thieves' toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=59) --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +30 **Will** +26 **HP** 225 **Dramatic Entrance** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Trigger** The flamboyant thief rolls initiative; **Effect** The flamboyant thief draws all eyes to them. They attempt a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check, comparing the result against the Will DC of any number of creatures within 120 feet. Each creature the thief succeeds against is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the thief until the end of the thief's next turn.
**I Say When I'm Here** When any [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686), or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) magic would reveal the flamboyant thief, the thief becomes aware of it and can attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the magic with a counteract rank of 8th level and using their [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) as their counteract modifier.
**Nimble Dodge **Trigger** The thief is targeted with a melee or ranged attack by an attacker it can see** **Effect** The thief gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _dagger_ +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+11 piercing plus spectacular attack **Melee** fist +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+11 bludgeoning plus spectacular attack **Ranged** _dagger_ +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+11 piercing plus spectacular attack **Dancing Dagger** The flamboyant thief can Step, attempt a melee dagger Strike, and attempt a ranged dagger Strike, taking the actions in any order. Both Strikes count toward the thief's multiple attack penalty, but it doesn't increase until after both attacks. **Dramatic Exit** The flamboyant thief throws down their smoke ball, then [Hides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404), then Sneaks up to three times with a +2 circumstance bonus to their [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks. **Spectacular Attack** All the flamboyant thief's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage or 6d6 if the target is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the thief. After the thief Strikes a creature, that creature becomes fascinated with the thief until the end of the thief's next turn.
Calling Cards Flamboyant thieves love to build personas for themselves and gain an infamous reputation. Their choice of calling card is the primary way they accomplish this. It must be something unique and difficult to replicate to prevent pretenders. Some calling cards may include the feather of a [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137), a rare flower, an intricate clockwork toy, or a custom playing card.
name: Flamboyant Thief

Pilgrims traverse the lands spurred on by some holy reason, be it to visit a sacred place or follow a prophetic vision. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 13 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8 [Pilgrim of Irori](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3437) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 28 **Perception** +5 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +5, [Irori Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +6, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +5, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +4, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +5 **Str** +1 **Dex** +0 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Ambulatory Oration** Creatures that engage in conversation with the pilgrim gain a +1 circumstance bonus to all [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks and [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) checks for 4 hours related to any topics discussed with the pilgrim.
**Path of the Faithful** The pilgrim can use their [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) modifier instead of their [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) modifier to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) or [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) as long as the pilgrim includes their religious teachings. **Items** [Explorer's Clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2), [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of [Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=286), walking stick (functions as a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)) --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +4 **Ref** +4 **Will** +7 **HP** 9 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** staff +5 ([two-hand 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +5 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d6+1 bludgeoning **Ranged** rock +4 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning

Abbots are often the heads of religious institutions less focused on spellcasting, such as orphanages, religious schools, or charities. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Abbot of Abadar](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3439) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 28 **Perception** +7 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +6, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +8, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +7 **Str** +1 **Dex** +1 **Con** -2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Religious Specialist** For encounters involving religious debates or conflicts of doctrine, the abbot is a 9th-level challenge.
**True Faith** The abbot uses lessons from scripture to foil others trying to deceive them. They can use their [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) modifier to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) instead of Perception, and their Religion DC instead of their Perception DC against attempts to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to them. **Items** [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (10 bolts), griffon cane (functions as a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)), [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=277) --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +4 **Will** +11 **HP** 15 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** griffon cane +6 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +6 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Melee** crossbow +6 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing **Divine Protection** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The abbot beseeches their deity to protect someone in their charge, attempting a DC 25 Religion check. If it succeeds, a divine [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660) spell affects one of the abbot's allies within 60 feet. The Will DC is 17
name: Abbot of Abadar

markdown:
Blasphemers spread messages contrary to the tenets of their faith, often out of the belief that the gods are specifically targeting them to spread this message. In some cultures, such as Nidal, this is a heretical crime and can send a blasphemer on the run from the law. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Blasphemer of Zon-Kuthon](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3441) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 29 **Perception** +8 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=21) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +9, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +7, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +7, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +6, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +7 **Str** +3 **Dex** +1 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Twisted Faith** When attempting a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) skill check, the blasphemer can roll [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) instead, so long as they have an intelligent creature around as a witness. If the creature is a follower of the blasphemer's faith, the blasphemer receives a +2 circumstance bonus to the check. **Items** [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69), [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=296), [spiked chain](/Equipment.aspx?ID=11) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +7 **Will** +10 **HP** 35 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** spiked chain +9 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)), **Damage** 1d8+5 slashing **Melee** fist +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** hand crossbow +7 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **False Blessing** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The blasphemer attempts a DC 15 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check to attempt to cast the 1st-rank spell their deity grants to clerics ([_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) for [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=296)). The spell must take 1, 2, or 3 actions to Cast. The blasphemer can use twisted faith to roll [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) instead if they have a witness, as normal.
**Critical Success** The blasphemer successfully Casts the Spell, then is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) with a value equal to the number of actions the spell takes – 1.
**Success** As critical success, plus the blasphemer takes 1d6 mental damage.
**Failure** The blasphemer fails to Cast the Spell and takes 1d6 mental damage.
**Critical Failure** The blasphemer fails to Cast the Spell, takes 2d6 mental damage, and is stunned 1.
name: Blasphemer of Zon-Kuthon

<title level="1" pfs="">[Sibyl](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3443)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sibyls are oracular diviners who often fall deep into trances with their oracular curses to spur a frenzied mental state. In this trance, they connect with gods and spirits, albeit in a disorganized haze. Some false sibyls will use substances to try and attempt to bring on this same frantic connection, often with deadly results.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Sibyl.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +9; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +9, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +9, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +9, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +3

**Con** -1

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Induce Awe** The sibyl can use [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) instead of [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) or [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395).

**Items**
bundles of herbs, [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +6 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +12 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 40

</row>

**Foresight** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The sibyl becomes the target of a spell with the [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [prediction](/Traits.aspx?ID=674), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686), or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) trait; **Effect** The sibyl's oracular awareness alerts them to danger. They gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their saving throw or AC against the spell.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 piercing plus 1d6 spirit

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning plus 1d6 spirit

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+4 piercing plus 1d6 spirit

**Divine Spontaneous Spells** DC 19, attack +11
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Haunting Hymn](/Spells.aspx?ID=1999), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582)
- **1st**
[Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Concordant Choir](/Spells.aspx?ID=1979), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603) (4 slots)
- **2nd**
[Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [Sudden Blight](/Spells.aspx?ID=2033) (3 slots)

**Oracle Focus Spells 1 Focus Point,** DC 19
- **2nd**
[Brain Drain](/Spells.aspx?ID=2084)

**Divine Frenzy** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** The sibyl isn't [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) or in a frenzy; **Effect** The sibyl enters into a divine frenzy that lasts 1 minute. The sibyl can't voluntarily stop frenzying. While in a divine frenzy, the sibyl takes a –2 penalty to Perception checks and Will saves and gains a +2 status bonus to their spell DC and spell attack modifier. During a divine frenzy, the sibyl can't use actions with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait unless they're Casting a Spell or [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301). The frenzy lasts for 1 minute, until the sibyl falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or the encounter ends, whichever comes first. The sibyl can't voluntarily end the frenzy.

</column>

Zealots ferret out plots against their religion and seek justice for their church's followers. This zealot serves [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278), but others might serve [Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=277), [Calistria](/Deities.aspx?ID=279), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285), [Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=289), [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), or [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=296). They often ride a [war horse](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3060) (Monster Core 201) wearing light [barding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2778). To depict a zealot's mount, add this horse to the encounter as an additional monster with its own actions, adjusting the encounter's [XP budget](/Rules.aspx?ID=2717) accordingly. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Zealot of Asmodeus](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3444) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 31 **Perception** +9 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +12, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +7 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 **Items** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows), [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12), [Mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 22 (24 with shield raised) **Fort** +9 **Ref** +7 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 **[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)**
**Swear Vengeance** **Trigger** A creature the zealot can see damages a follower of [Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=278) other than the zealot **Effect** The zealot is affected by a [_sure strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) spell. If the zealot makes an attack roll against anyone other than the triggering creature, the _sure strike_ ends with no effect. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** mace +12 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 1d8+4 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning **Ranged** composite shortbow +9 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 19, attack +11 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Runic Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1658), [Spirit Link](/Spells.aspx?ID=1686) - **2nd** [Cleanse Affliction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×4), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [Share Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=1669) **Channel Smite** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Cost** the zealot expends a [_harm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) spell; **Effect** The zealot makes a melee Strike. If it hits, they damage the target with a 1-action _harm_ spell. The target automatically gets a failure (or a critical failure if the Strike was a critical hit). The spell doesn't have the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait when cast this way. **Deadly Simplicity** The zealot deals 1d8 damage with their mace instead of 1d6.
name: Zealot of Asmodeus

<title level="1" pfs="">[Priest](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3445)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cloistered priests safeguard their temples and communities. They are the stalwart keepers of their god's tenets, devoted to spreading the word. Their guidance or healing services come at the cost of a donation.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Priest_of_Sarenrae.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Priest of Sarenrae](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3445)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 32

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +3

**Con** +1

**Int** +0

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [scimitar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=393)_, [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +11 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +16 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 80

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_scimitar_ +14 ([Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Divine Prepared Spells** DC 24, attack +16
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744)
- **1st**
[Infuse Vitality](/Spells.aspx?ID=1574), [Breathe Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457), [Spirit Link](/Spells.aspx?ID=1686)
- **2nd**
[Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [Revealing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653), [Spiritual Armament](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687)
- **3rd**
[Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×5), [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (×2)

**Cleric Domain Spells** DC 24, 1 Focus Point
- **3rd**
[Dazzling Flash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1836)

**Divine Rituals** DC 24

- **2nd**
[Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8)

**Healing Hands**   When the priest casts [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), they roll d10s instead of d8s.

**Steady Spellcasting**   If another creature's reaction [disrupts](/Rules.aspx?ID=2342) the priest's spellcasting action, the priest attempts a DC 15 flat check. If the priest succeeds, their action isn't disrupted.

</column>

markdown:
Champions are bastions of their deities' virtues and are living beacons for their causes. Those who walk these paths must adhere to the tenets of their patron deity or risk losing their abilities altogether. The champion depicted here follows the example of [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=293), embodying the spirit of inspiring beauty and fighting for the cause of peace. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Champion of Shelyn](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3446) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 32 **Perception** +15 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +16, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (10 bolts), [Half Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=12), _+1 [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=375)_, [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=293) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +12 **Will** +15 **HP** 120 **Champion's Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 15 feet. Any follower of [Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=293) in the aura knows the champion is a champion of Shelyn. At the end of the champion's turn, each ally in the aura reduces its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) value by 1. The aura can be suppressed or resumed with a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait, and ends if the champion falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95).
**Champion's Courage** When the champion becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), they reduce the condition value by 1 (to a minimum of 0).
**Liberating Step** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** An enemy damages, [grabs](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77), or [restrains](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) the champion's ally, and both are in the champion's aura; **Effect** The champion frees an ally from restraint. If the trigger was an ally taking damage, the ally gains resistance 10 to all damage against the triggering damage.
The ally can attempt to break free of effects grabbing, restraining, [immobilizing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), or [paralyzing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) them. They either attempt a new save against one such effect that allows a save or attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) from one effect as a free action. Whether or not it needed to escape, the ally can then Step as a free action if it's able to move. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _glaive_ +18 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d8+10 slashing plus 1d6 persistent vitality **Melee** fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** crossbow +14 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d8+6 piercing **Champion Devotion Spells** DC 22, 2 Focus Points - **4th** [Lay on Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=2047), [Protector's Sacrifice](/Spells.aspx?ID=1832) **Blessed Weapon** If a champion's glaive Strike is a critical hit, the weapon deals an additional 1d6 [persistent vitality damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and they can force the target to [move](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet in a direction of their choice. **Smite** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The champion chooses one enemy they can see. Their Strikes against that enemy gain a +4 status bonus to damage, or +8 if the target is [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521).
This benefit lasts until the start of the champion's next turn, but if the target takes a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the champion or one of their allies, the duration is extended until the end of the target's next turn (this can be extended indefinitely if the target keeps taking hostile actions on subsequent rounds).
Champion Causes [Champions](/Classes.aspx?ID=4) represent the martial forces tied to the various faiths all around Golarion. You can customize a champion to a different deity by swapping in their deity's favored weapon, selecting an appropriate blessing of the devoted, and choosing a cause fitting that deity's sanctification options. Use that cause to determine the champion's sanctification and champion's reaction.
name: Champion of Shelyn

markdown:
High priests are the leaders of larger churches and similar religious establishments, watching over the lower-ranking clergy and ensuring the surrounding community is taken care of. This can be a highly political position, as the leader of the faith in an area has a powerful influence over everyday citizens. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [High Priest of Pharasma](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3447) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 33 **Perception** +20 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +18, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +26 **Str** +1 **Dex** +2 **Con** -1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Religious Specialist** For encounters involving religious debates, church politics, and conflicts of doctrine, the high priest is a 13th-level challenge. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_, [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69) (20 bolts), [Healer's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727), [_holy water_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3001) (4), [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), [religious text](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2746) of [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290), [_scroll of cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) (4th rank) --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +17 **Will** +21 **HP** 150 **Resistances** [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 5 **Steward of the Faithful** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) 30 feet. Each ally in the aura who worships [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=290) gains resistance 5 to void and a +1 status bonus to Will saves, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks, and [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks.
**Unshakable Faith** During a religious debate, clash of church politics, or similar conflict, the high priest gains a +4 circumstance bonus to Perception check to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) and to their Perception DC against attempt to lie to them. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _dagger_ +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+7 piercing plus 1d10 spirit **Melee** fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning plus 1d10 spirit **Ranged** hand crossbow +17 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+7 piercing plus 1d10 spirit **Ranged** _dagger_ +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+7 piercing plus 1d10 spirit **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 28, attack +20 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Vitality Lash](/Spells.aspx?ID=1744) - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603), [Spirit Link](/Spells.aspx?ID=1686) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [Darkvision](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481), [Status](/Spells.aspx?ID=1690) - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Ghostly Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1544), [Heroism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555) - **4th** [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (×2), [Vital Beacon](/Spells.aspx?ID=1743) - **5th** [Breath of Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=1456), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×5), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) **Cleric Domain Spells** DC 28, 2 Focus Points - **5th** [Death's Call](/Spells.aspx?ID=1792), [Eradicate Undeath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1793) **Rituals** DC 28 - **2nd** [Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8), [Heartbond](/Rituals.aspx?ID=33) - **4th** [Atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2), [Rest Eternal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=36) - **5th** [Resurrect](/Rituals.aspx?ID=20) **Healing Hands** When the high priest casts [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), they roll d10s instead of d8s. **Restorative Channel** The high priest can sacrifice one prepared heal spell to instead cast [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [_clear mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679), or [_sure footing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1708) at the same spell rank.
name: High Priest of Pharasma

markdown:
Deities and their religions are only as strong as the belief of their faithful. Traveling priests spread word to all corners of Golarion, building the numbers devoted to their denomination through their journeys. No matter where they may be headed or found, a traveling priest is likely to be healing someone with a spell, delivering a message, or simply trying to ensure their faith is spread. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Traveling Priest of Desna](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3448) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 34 **Perception** +19 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +17, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +19 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Path of the Faithful** The pilgrim can evangelize their religious teachings to use their [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) modifier instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) or [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392).
**Traveler's Lesson** Creatures that engage in conversation with the traveling priest gain a +2 circumstance bonus to all [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks and [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) checks for 4 hours related to any topics discussed with the traveling priest. **Items** [_shining symbol_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3109), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=399)_ --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +19 **Will** +19 **HP** 140 **Messenger's Amnesty** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A traveling priest with a message to deliver is continually protected by a DC 25 [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660) spell. If the traveling priest breaks the _sanctuary_, the effect returns if the traveling priest ceases hostility for 10 minutes. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _starknife_ +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+8 piercing **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning **Melee** _starknife_ +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d4+8 piercing **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 27, attack +19 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **1st** [Alarm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439), [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) - **2nd** [Create Food](/Spells.aspx?ID=1475), [Environmental Endurance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1517), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1674) - **3rd** [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503), [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557), [Safe Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659) - **4th** [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675), [Spiritual Armament](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **5th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×5), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Cleric Domain Spells** DC 27, 2 Focus Points - **5th** [Agile Feet](/Spells.aspx?ID=1838), [Traveler's Transit](/Spells.aspx?ID=1839) **Blessing of Travel** If the traveling priest takes an action with the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) trait, their Strikes deal an extra 2d8 spirit damage until the end of their turn. **Zealous Rush** **Trigger** The traveling priest casts a spell that takes 1 or more actions and affects only them; **Effect** The traveling priest Strides up to 10 feet, or up to their full Speed if the triggering spell took 2 actions or more to cast.
name: Traveling Priest of Desna

markdown:
Followers of [Rovagug](/Deities.aspx?ID=291) must usually conceal their devotion to the Rough Beast, hiding in remote cave complexes or abandoned ruins, but they emerge when they sense weakness, gathering together into a destructive, homicidal mob to hunt down priests of rival deities or slaughter entire towns. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Rancorous Priesthood](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3449) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 35 **Perception** +21 **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +21, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +22 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +17 **Will** +22 **HP** 195 (4 segments) **Weaknesses** area damage 10, splash damage 10 **[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet, [troop movement](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=85) **Divine Spontaneous Spells** DC 30, attack +22 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498) - **5th** [Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496), [Noise Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [Shadow Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666) (3 slots) **Cleric Domain Spells** DC 30, attack +22, 2 Focus Points - **6th** [Cry of Destruction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1794), [Destructive Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1795) **Rituals** DC 30 - **4th** [Blight](/Rituals.aspx?ID=4) (doesn't require secondary casters) **Troop Spellcasting** When the rancorous priesthood Casts a Spell, their constituent members combine their efforts into casting a more powerful version of the spell than any one member could achieve alone. When Casting a Spell that has an area of a burst, cone, or line and doesn't have a duration, increase the area of that spell. Add 5 feet to the radius of a burst that normally has a radius of at least 10 feet (a burst with a smaller radius is not affected). Add 5 feet to the length of a cone or line that is normally 15 feet long or smaller, and add 10 feet to the length of a larger cone or line. **Wild Swing** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The members of the mob wildly swing their weapons in a chaotic attack at each enemy in a 5-foot emanation with a DC 25 basic Reflex save. The damage depends on the number of actions.
1d12+2 slashing damage
2d12+9 slashing damage
3d12+9 slashing damage
Troops With Spells Though [Rovagug's](/Deities.aspx?ID=291) followers are the most common, other deities might have rancorous priesthoods. You can use these statistics to represent other devotee troops capable of casting spells by replacing the spells in the list, changing the domain spells, and altering the sanctification. Note that the spell slots are 1 below the maximum rank a spellcaster of their level could normally cast. Heightened lower-level spells can be a good fit, representing the combined effect of several lower-rank spells cast at the same time.
name: Rancorous Priesthood

The deific champion is the paragon of champions, representing the truest form of devotion and dedication to a deity. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Deific Champion of Iomedae](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3450) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 36 **Perception** +19 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +23 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Blessed Shield** In the deific champion's hands, a shield gains the [_moderate reinforcing rune_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2811), giving it Hardness 8, 84 HP, and BT 42.
**Deific Reactions** At the start of each of their turns, the deific champion gains an additional reaction they can only use to make a Reactive Strike or to Shield Block. **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=425)_ (20 bolts), _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_, _[potion of flying](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2948)_, [religious symbol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2745) of [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285), [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=3) --- **AC** 33 (35 with shield raised) **Fort** +23 **Ref** +19 **Will** +22 **HP** 220 **Champion's Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) 15 feet. Any follower of [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285) in the aura knows the champion is a champion of Iomedae. At the end of the champion's turn, each ally in the aura reduces its [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) value by 1. The aura can be suppressed or resumed with a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait, and ends if the champion falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95).
**Champion's Courage** When the champion becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), they reduce the condition value by 1 (to a minimum of 0).
**Exalted Retributive Strike** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** An enemy damages the deific champion's ally, and both are in the deific champion's aura; **Effect** The ally gains resistance 14 to all damage against the triggering damage. If the enemy is within reach, the deific champion makes a melee Strike against it. Each ally in the champion's aura can spend a reaction to Strike the target with a –5 penalty.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=75)**
**Will Not Fall** Trigger The deific champion's Hit Points are reduced to 0 for the first time that day; Effect The champion presses on, refusing to fail their god. They remain standing with 25 Hit Points. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** _longsword_ +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing **Melee** fist +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+13 bludgeoning **Ranged** _crossbow_ +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 2d8+5 piercing **Champion Devotion Spells** DC 30, 2 Focus Points - **6th** [Champion's Sacrifice](/Spells.aspx?ID=2050), [Lay on Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=2047) **Will Not Falter** **Effect** The deific champion declares their devotion to their deity and their cause. They Stride, then make a melee Strike. If the Strike hits an enemy, all allies within their champion's aura gain a +2 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) until the start of the deific champion's next turn.
name: Deific Champion of Iomedae

markdown:
Through direct intervention in the mortal world, a deity creates a deific vessel to do their will, whether from a willing servant or through possession, a contract, or a curse. A candle to the flame of their progenitor, a divine vessel burns fast and quickly extinguishes. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Deific Vessel of Urgathoa](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3451) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 37 **Perception** +27; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +26, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +31, [Undead Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +33 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Mark of Fate** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A creature that slays the deific vessel must succeed at a DC 35 Will save or be visibly marked as anathema to [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=295). It gains weakness 10 to [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to Charisma-based skill checks against followers of Urgathoa. Creatures attempting to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391) about or [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the marked creature gain a +2 circumstance bonus to their checks. The mark can't be hidden and can be removed only by participating in an [_atone_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=108) ritual led by a worshipper of Urgathoa who is 12th level or higher. **Items** _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [explorer's clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=39)_, [Scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=39) --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +24 **Will** +26 **HP** 300 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 15 **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 15 **Void Tendrils** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) 30 feet. When a creature in the aura would be healed by a vitality effect, the healing is reduced by 15 and the deific vessel regains 15 Hit Points.
**Limited Lifespan** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The deific vessel takes 25 damage at the end of its turn if it Cast a Spell, used Borrow Time, or made a Strike that turn. This damage ignores resistance.
**Shattered Vessel** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) When the deific vessel dies, the divine power barely contained within their form explodes outward, dealing 6d8 spirit damage to each creature in a 30-foot emanation with a DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** _decaying frost scythe_ +28 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d10+11 slashing plus 1d6 cold and 1d4 void **Melee** _decaying frost fist_ +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 3d4+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold and 1d4 void **Ranged** grave pulse +27 ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [Death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [ranged 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)), **Damage** 3d6 cold plus 2d8 void **Divine Innate Spells** DC 39, attack +31 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **4th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (at will) - **6th** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727), [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735), [Zealous Conviction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1760) - **7th** [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495), [Eclipse Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1508), [Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519) - **8th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×4), [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=1595) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Borrow Time** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The vessel chooses two different creatures in their void tendrils aura. Each one must be either [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) or the vessel themself. One target loses 25 HP and the other regains that many HP. If a target is unwilling, it can negate the transfer with a successful DC 39 Fortitude save. **Grave Chill** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The vessel's unarmed attacks and scythe gain the _+2 [decaying](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2835) [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829)_ runes when used by the vessel, and their Strikes gain the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) trait.
Consequences Of Magnitude Directly interceding in the mortal plane opens deities up to direct consequences from other divine entities and the very forces of fate itself. The moment a deific vessel comes into existence, a clock begins ticking down, ready to change the fate of all involved in the creation of this powerful entity. The gods and their heralds, from their unassailable positions, face far fewer consequences than the relatively unprotected vessels themselves.
name: Deific Vessel of Urgathoa

<title level="1" pfs="">[Beggar](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3452)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wherever there is poverty, there are beggars. Beggars are truly the downtrodden of society, as folks who have been cast out into the streets due to a variety of setbacks or the weight of circumstance. Some beggars work for underworld organizations as lookouts, spies, or even hired muscle on the cheap—sometimes by choice, but often not.

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Although their escapades might be notorious, few urchins are thrill-seekers. For some urchins, when begging alone isn't enough to fill their bellies, theft becomes a viable survival tactic. Many criminal groups also use urchins as spies and messengers, while training them to become future pickpockets, burglars, and foot soldiers.

**Drunken Flailing** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The drunkard is raging; **Effect** The drunkard attempts two fist Strikes, each against a different creature.

**Drunken Rage** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** The drunkard is drunk, and isn't [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) or raging; **Effect** The drunkard flies into a drunken rage. They gain 6 temporary Hit Points that last until the drunken rage ends. While raging, they deal 4 additional damage with melee attacks and take a –1 penalty to AC. The drunkard can't use [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) actions except [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301). The rage lasts for 1 minute, until the drunkard falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), or until the drunkard sobers up. The drunkard can't voluntarily stop raging. Once the rage ends, the drunkard can't gain temporary HP from this action for 1 minute.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Gunsmith](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3456)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Even though every gunslinger learns the basics of maintaining and repairing firearms as a part of their training, few would question the expertise of a master gunsmith regarding the weapons by which they live and die. Although professional gunsmiths are rarely found outside of settlements where firearms are common, such as Alkenstar or Dongun Hold, true masters of this specialized craft tend to quickly build reputations for themselves that extend well beyond the borders of whatever region they call home.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

**Languages**

**Skills**

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +0

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Artisan's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703) (gunsmithing), [Dueling Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=201) (2, 20 rounds)

</column>

---

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 16

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dueling pistol +8 ([Concealable](/Traits.aspx?ID=560), [Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), [fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+2 piercing

**Crafty Reload**   The gunsmith can Interact to reload a firearm without a free hand if they have a firearm in each hand. In addition, each time the gunsmith reloads a firearm, they can attempt a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check against the hard DC for the firearm's level (DC 17 for a [dueling pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=520)). On a success, they gain a +1 circumstance bonus on the next attack roll they make with that firearm before the start of their next turn.

**Firearm Sabotage** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The gunsmith is wielding or holding a one-handed firearm and has a free hand; **Effect** The gunsmith deftly makes a minor modification to a firearm that can be detected with a Perception check opposed by the gunsmith's [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) DC. If the sabotage is not reversed with a successful Crafting check against the gunsmith's Crafting DC, the firearm automatically misfires the next time it is used (the flat check is an automatic failure; see the Misfires sidebar).

</column>

markdown:
No one knows how to get the most out of their equipment better than a mechanic. A mechanic can repair just about any mechanical device you put in front of them with aplomb. However, they can also push the device to achieve more than you ever could have hoped for, provided you do not mind a small explosion every now and again. Don't get distracted by their impressive work, or you might be blindsided by a fast-swinging wrench. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Mechanic](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3457) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 42 **Perception** +5 **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +7, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +16, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +6 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +0 **Mechanical Repair** The mechanic is trained in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), but a master in Crafting for mechanical devices, siege weapons, and vehicles. They can [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) in 1 minute instead of 10 minutes, or in 3 actions for a mechanical device, siege weapon, or vehicle.
**Mechanical Specialist** For encounters involving mechanical repair, the mechanic is an 8th-level challenge. **Items** [Arbalest](/Weapons.aspx?ID=432) (20 bolts), heavy wrench, [Repair Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747) --- **AC** 14 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +6 **Will** +3 **HP** 22 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** heavy wrench +7 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 1d6+4 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning **Ranged** arbalest +8 ([Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=544), [range increment 110 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d10 piercing **Risky Upgrade** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The mechanic pushes a mechanical device, siege weapon, or vehicle pasts its regular limits with a temporary upgrade chosen from the list below. An item can have only one risky upgrade at a time. If an item has an upgrade at the start of the mechanic's turn, the mechanic must attempt a DC 5 flat check. (These flat checks continue even if the mechanic is dead or otherwise can't take turns.) On a failure, the item explodes, dealing damage equal to the item's level to all adjacent creatures and ending the upgrade.
  • **Overheat Weapons** If the item would deal damage, it deals an additional 1d6 fire damage. This increases to 2d6 if the item is 8th level or higher.
  • **Pressured Plating** The item gains a +3 status bonus to its Hardness and gains temporary Hit Points equal to double its level that last for 10 minutes.
  • **Propelled Boost** If the item has a Speed, the item gains a +15-foot status bonus to Speed.

markdown:
With daring and charm, a driver is a professional operator of mechanical vehicles. Their skill and recklessness give them a reputation that puts even the quickest horse riders to shame. A driver's vehicle becomes an extension of themselves, allowing the driver to perform near-impossible feats of maneuverability. Drivers have other tricks up their sleeve, as their charm is undeniable. When needed, they can talk, lie, or frighten their way out of a bumpy situation in and out of a vehicle. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Driver](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3458) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 43 **Perception** +8 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +7, [Driving Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +8, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +8, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +7, [Piloting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Driving Specialist** For encounters involving driving, the driver is an 8th-level challenge. Rules for vehicles appear [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3116).
**Express Driver** A driver can attempt a [Driving Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check to increase a vehicle's travel Speed when calculating the value for a day. The DC is determined by the GM but is typically based on the vehicle's piloting DC or the difficulty of traversing the environment, whichever is harder. On a success, increase the vehicle's travel Speed by half.
**Skilled Driver** The driver gains a +10 circumstance bonus to any skill check involved in driving a vehicle, and is considered a master in the skill for such checks. This bonus also applies to any initiative roll while the driver is piloting a vehicle. **Items** crowbar (functions as a [pick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=389)), [Hand Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=69) (10 bolts), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +8 **Will** +6 +6 status to all defenses while driving **HP** 28 **Vehicle Block** **Requirements** The driver is driving a vehicle; **Trigger** The driver would take damage from an attack or from a damaging effect that requires a Reflex save; **Effect** With swift steering, the driver puts the bulk of the vehicle in between themself and the problem, causing the vehicle to take the damage instead of the driver. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** crowbar +7 ([fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing **Melee** fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Ranged** hand crossbow +10 ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+2 piercing **Sideswipe** **Requirements** The driver is taking a [Drive](/Actions.aspx?ID=483) action with a vehicle and moves the vehicle adjacent to a creature; **Effect** All creatures adjacent to the vehicle take the vehicle's collision damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against the vehicle's collision DC. The vehicle continues to move normally after the Sideswipe.
Aviator Not all drivers limit themselves to the earth. Aviators are drivers who specialize in driving mechanical flying machines. Many see such drivers as being reckless, as they are often unsatisfied with simply flying from point A to point B. Instead, they take full advantage of the freedom of the sky to find the most exciting path available, which often involves dubiously named stunts.
name: Driver

<title level="1" pfs="">[Artillerist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3459)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The maintenance and operation of a siege weapon is the highest form of art for the artillerist. They are second to none in terms of reliability and speed due to years of experience and training. Their mastery is such that they often construct smaller models of their preferred weapon to mount on their shoulders. Many will insist this is in case of an emergency, but they often truly enjoy crafting, designing, and perfecting their personal piece of artillery. Traditionally, artillerists are used on both side of a siege. Massive weapons are both fired towards the walls and from the walls. In such battles, artillerists are invaluable. However, artillerists are seen in other places outside of massive wars. For example, artillerists are often employed on ships to manage their cannons and harpoons.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

**Siege Acumen** The artillerist is permanently [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89). They can use this extra action only to [Aim, Load, or Launch](/Rules.aspx?ID=1623) a siege weapon.

**Items**
[Artisan's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703) (siege weaponry), [Dueling Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=201) (20 rounds), [Light Hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=384), [Repair Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747)

light hammer +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),

fist +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),

**Bombard** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The artillerist activates a shoulder-mounted artillery piece to launch an explosive shell up to 120 feet away that explodes in 10-foot burst. Creatures within the burst take 2d6 piercing damage and 2d6 fire damage with a DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The artillerist can't use Bombard again until they reload the artillery with 2 Interact actions; these actions don't have to be consecutive.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Siege Weapons</title>

The artillerist is meant to pair with siege weapons, and engineers in general often pair well with these large instruments of war. Siege weapons usually work best with multiple NPCs crewing them. Rules for siege weapons can be found [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=1622).
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Toymaker](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3460)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The whimsy of a toymaker is only matched by their ruthlessness when cornered. Most of their creations are designed solely for the enjoyment of others. However, every toymaker knows—whether through failed designs or intentional creations—how to turn their toys into dangerous weapons. They are often hesitant to use toys in such ways, and more often than not, they will attempt to solve problems with diplomacy first.

 Some toymakers have fully turned to the profession of making deadly toys. They often have dangerous patrons or nefarious intentions. Some use dangerous toys as a means of vigilantism, while others use them as a means to sneak weapons into guarded areas.

</column>

**Punchout Bolts**   The toymaker's crossbow bolts are specially constructed with heavy, sap-like heads instead of piercing tips. Strikes with these bolts deal bludgeoning damage instead of piercing and have the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) trait. In addition, a creature hit by one must succeed a DC 20 Fortitude saving throw or be [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet back (or 20 feet on a critical failure).

**Scatter Blocks** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The toymaker throws out a handful of toy building blocks of various sizes 20 feet away in a 5-foot burst. The area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=454). A creature that moves on the ground through the area takes 1 piercing damage for every square of that area it moves into.

**Wind-Up Soldier** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The toymaker releases a wind-up soldier that Strides 15 feet in a straight line. Whenever the soldier moves adjacent to a creature or a creature moves into a space adjacent to the soldier, the creature takes 2d8 slashing damage with a DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save as the soldier wildly slashes its sword. A creature can take damage from the wind-up soldier only once per round. At the start of each of the toymaker's turns, the solder Strides 15 feet further along the same path. The soldier falls apart after it moves three times.

</column>

While most engineers take immense pride in their work constructing something that may survive many lifetimes, the demolitionist takes pride in destroying such pompous things in the most spectacular way possible. Every design has a flaw, and that flaw usually involves large quantities of explosives. Demolitionists are often pragmatic and calculated, taking great care to destroy whatever lies before them as efficiently as possible. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Demolitionist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3461) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 45 **Perception** +10 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +9, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +12, [Explosive Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +10, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +11 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +0 **Cha** +0 **Items** bag of explosives, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [Light Mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=5) --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +13 **Will** +6 **HP** 60 **Resistances** fire 5 **Explosive Demise** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) When the demolitionist is reduced to 0 Hit Points while they have any explosives still in their bag, the remaining explosives detonate, unleashing an explosion of fire upon all creatures in a 30-foot emanation. Each creature in the area takes 3d6 fire damage with a DC 19 Reflex save.
**Replenish Explosives** The demolitionist can replenish their stock of explosives with 4 hours of downtime. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** light mace +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning **Plant Mine** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The demolitionist plants a mine in an adjacent square. If a creature moves onto a space with a mine, the mine explodes. This deals 3d8 fire damage to the creature with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The demolitionist can use 2 actions to Plant a Mine to hide the mine, granting it a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) DC of 21. Creatures that didn't see the mine as it was planted must actively search for it (using the [Search](/Actions.aspx?ID=2630) activity while exploring or the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action in an encounter). **Toss Dynamite** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The demolitionist quickly throws a stick of dynamite up to 20 feet away that explodes in 5-foot burst. Creatures within the burst take 4d4 fire damage with a DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. **Wall Charge** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) the demolitionist plants a powerful wall charge on a flat surface such as a door or wall. Once the charge is planted, it explodes after 1 minute, dealing 60 fire damage to the surface and ignoring up to 15 of the surface's Hardness. The explosive also deals 5d6 fire damage to creatures within 30 feet of the explosive with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save DC 25.
name: Demolitionist

markdown:
Few are as prepared to be unprepared as a gadgeteer, who are masters at building seemingly impossible creations out of almost anything. They're always useful to have when a plan inevitably goes wrong because they can quickly craft the perfect tool needed to get out of even the stickiest predicament. Every now and again, someone tries to hire a gadgeteer to perfect a single design to replicate it and make more permanent versions of their thrown-together gadgets. This effort is often in vain, as even the most talented gadgeteers are unlikely to perfectly replicate a design they made in the moment, even with the same materials. In fact, when put on the spot, they will likely create a functional gadget differently every time, even if they have access to the same material. This process makes duplication nearly impossible. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Gadgeteer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3462) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 46 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +16, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +12, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +14 **Str** +1 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Gadget Specialist** For encounters involving crafting gadgets, the gadgeteer is a 9th-level challenge. **Items** bag of junk, [Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (10 bolts), heavy wrench (functions as a [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=362)), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4) --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +16 **Will** +14 **HP** 95 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+7 bludgeoning **Melee** heavy wrench +13 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), **Damage** 1d6+7 bludgeoning **Ranged** crossbow +16 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d8+6 piercing **Create Gadget** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The gadgeteer uses their bag of junk and nearby scraps to create one of the following gadgets. Gadgets created this way fall apart after a single use or after 1 hour, whichever happens first.
  • **Flash Bang** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The gadgeteer throws a flash bang up to 20 feet away that explodes in a 5-foot burst. Creatures in the burst must succeed a DC 24 Fortitude save or become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
  • **Glider** ([move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The gadgeteer leaps off a precipice with the glider in their hands. They fall only 60 feet per round, and for every 10 feet they fall, they can travel 5 feet forward.
  • **Makeshift Key** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The gadgeteer attempts to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412) with a +4 item bonus to the check.
  • **Recorder** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The gadgeteer records up to 25 spoken words on this device. Activating this gadget causes it to either repeat the recorded words once before falling apart or play the message on a loop for up to 10 minutes before falling apart.
  • **Shocking Rod** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) An adjacent creature takes 3d12 electricity damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save.
name: Gadgeteer

<title level="1" pfs="">[Rocketeer](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3463)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

It takes a very specific personality to strap on a tank filled with a highly flammable alchemical substance and set it afire to launch oneself into the sky, so it is perhaps unsurprising that most rocketeers are reckless and bombastic individuals who delight in the theatrics inherent in their craft. While the unpredictability of rocketeering devices and the high casualty rate among those who use them make such devices generally unsuitable for military applications, a few courageous souls have used them to become dashing folk heroes or performing daredevils, many of whose most memorable performances culminate in their own dramatic demises.
