**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Artisan's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703) (rocketry), flight suit (functions as [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)), heavy wrench (functions as a [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=362)), [moderate alchemist's fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75) (4), _+1 [slide pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=524)_ (20 rounds)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 23 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 85

</row>

**Fuel Tank Vulnerability** When the rocketeer is struck by a critical hit that deals piercing or fire damage, they must attempt a DC 5 flat check. On a failure, the rocketeer's fuel tank explodes, dealing 6d6 fire damage to the rocketeer and all creatures in a 20-foot emanation and knocking the rocketeer [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The rocketeer loses their fly Speed and can't use Explosive Liftoff, Mid-air Collision, or Rocketing Strafe until they repair their jet pack, which requires an appropriate set of [artisan's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3) and takes 2 hours.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
heavy wrench +14 ([Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)),
**Damage** 1d6+8 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_slide pistol_ +17 ([capacity 5](/Traits.aspx?ID=553), [Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), [fatal d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=597), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Explosive Liftoff** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Requirements** The rocketeer is standing on a horizontal surface; **Effect** The rocketeer unleashes the full strength of their jets to launch themself into the air, dealing 7d6 fire and bludgeoning damage to all creatures in a 15-foot emanation with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The rocketeer [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) twice, straight up into the air.

**Mid-air Collision** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The rocketeer [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) twice, then attempts to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) or [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) another flying creature. If they roll a success on the [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check, they get a critical success instead.

**Rocketing Strafe** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The rocketeer [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) and makes two melee Strikes at any point during that movement. Each Strike must target a different creature. The rocketeer can forgo the melee Strikes to instead make one slide pistol Strike at any point during that movement and Interact to select the next loaded chamber of their slide pistol; they can do these in either order. Any Strike made as part of a Rocketing Strafe deals an additional 2d6 damage and takes the normal multiple attack penalty.

</column>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The intricate clockwork prostheses known as [sterling dynamos](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=110) have become increasingly common in recent years, particularly in havens of technological progress like Dongun Hold and the city of Absalom, but a few specialized engineers have elevated their dedication to research and development of such devices to an entirely new level. These individuals seek to achieve bodily perfection through augmentation, replacing their own limbs with advanced and sometimes untested prototypes that grant them a wide variety of abilities.

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
modular prosthesis +20,
**Damage** 2d8+12 and see Modular Prostheses

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dragon mouth pistol +18 ([Concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=846), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [scatter 5 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=848)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Extend Arms** <actions string="Single Action" />  The dynamo extends their collapsible steel arms, giving them both a [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684) of 20 feet with all melee attacks. However, the dynamo becomes [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) and can't use the Interact action. The dynamo can Dismiss this ability.

**Extend Legs** <actions string="Single Action" />  The dynamo rises into the air on 10-foot-tall telescoping steel legs. While their legs are extended, the dynamo gains a +10-foot status bonus to land Speed and ignores any [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) granted by barriers less than 10 feet tall. However, the dynamo becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) and can't use the [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), or [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) actions. The dynamo can Dismiss this ability.

**Modular Prostheses** <actions string="Single Action" />  The dynamo configures one or both of their mechanical prosthetic hands into a specific configuration. Each configuration deals a specific damage type and has its own weapon traits: fist (bludgeoning; [free-hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=613)), gaff hook (piercing; [grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=619)), impact driver (bludgeoning; [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694)), or spinning blade (slashing; [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)). The dynamo can alternatively transform a hand into a steel shield with Hardness 8, HP 72, and BT 36. A [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60) prosthesis can't be reconfigured until repaired.

</column>

The heavy suit of mechanical metal armor a juggernaut wears is custom-built and highly complex and specialized to its wearer. Other creatures can't make use of the armor unless they have similar skill and customize it thoroughly. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Juggernaut](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3465) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 49 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26, [Engineering Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +2 **Integrated Weapon** A juggernaut's armor includes one integrated melee weapon, such as a diamond-tipped rotary saw blade, massive pneumatic drill, or heavy spiked gauntlet. The specifics don't change the damage dealt by its Strikes, but determines whether it deals bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage. A juggernaut with tools and a workshop can spend 2 hours to swap their armor's integrated weapon.
**Power Source** Juggernaut armor requires a power source built into the armor—such as a steam boiler, Stasian coil, or alchemical reservoir. This determines a damage type—cold, electricity, fire, or poison—for certain abilities. **Items** [Artisan's Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703) (blacksmithing), juggernaut armor, [Repair Toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747) --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +19 **Will** +21 **HP** 250 **Resistances** galvanized plating **Galvanized Plating** The juggernaut has resistance 10 to the damage type of the armor's power source.
**Self-Destruct** **Trigger** The juggernaut is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The juggernaut collapses and their armor emits a steady ticking sound. At the beginning of what would have been the juggernaut's next turn, the armor's power source explodes, destroying it completely and dealing 10d6 damage in a 30-foot emanation with a DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The explosion deals the damage type of the armor's power source. An adjacent creature can cancel the self-destruct sequence by succeeding at a DC 31 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411). --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** integrated weapon +27, **Damage** 3d8+12 and see integrated weapon **Melee** plated fist +27, **Damage** 3d4+14 bludgeoning **Energy Projector** ([Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528)) A juggernaut carries a powerful cannon-like projectile weapon that requires two hands to wield and deals 14d6 damage to all creatures in its area with a DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) save; the damage type, area, and save are based on the armor's power source, as listed below. Once activated, Energy Projector can't be used again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Cold** 30-foot cone of cold (Reflex)
  • **Electricity** 60-foot line of electricity (Reflex)
  • **Fire** 30-foot cone of fire (Reflex)
  • **Poison** 30-foot cone of poison gas (Fortitude)
**Jump Jets** ([Alchemical](/Traits.aspx?ID=528)) The juggernaut gains a Fly speed of 15 feet until the end of their current turn. If the juggernaut isn't on solid ground when they lose their fly Speed, they fall. After the effect ends, the juggernaut can't use Jump Jets again for 1 round. **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=81)** Medium or smaller, plated fist, DC 33
Juggernaut Rumors Legend has it that the first suit of juggernaut armor was built by a brilliant but misanthropic inventor who leveled his own workshop before embarking on a spree of indiscriminate destruction. Though the rampage quickly came to an end when his creation crashed through a wooden floor and became trapped in a basement, the story has inspired a new generation of machinists to diligently refine and improve on the original design.
name: Juggernaut

markdown:
Foragers know the areas they live and work in like no other. They can tell you exactly where to find a rare medicinal fern, when to harvest it, and how to use it; just don't expect them to be up-to-date on the latest town gossip. They spend as much time in the wilderness as they can, filling their baskets with a variety of useful plants. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Forager](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3467) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 52 **Perception** +7; (11 to notice flora and fauna) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Local Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +5, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +6, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +3, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Expert Subsistence** While using [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368), if the forager rolls any result worse than a success, they get a success. On a success, they can provide subsistence living for themselves and four additional creatures, and on a critical success, they can take care of twice as many creatures as on a success.
**Natural Specialist** For encounters involving [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49), the forager is a 3rd-level challenge. **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), pouches, wicker baskets --- **AC** 15 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +8 **Will** +8 **HP** 20 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dagger +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+1 piercing **Ranged** dagger +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+1 piercing **Melee** fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning **Ranged** fruit or vegetable +7 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning **Local Poison** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The forager coats their dagger in a diluted, locally sourced poison. Until the end of their turn, Strikes with their dagger deal an additional 2 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
name: Forager

markdown:
If there's a mystery of nature that requires firsthand evidence to solve, natural scientists collect that data. They spend months documenting and observing events and creatures in the natural world before returning to their academies, labs, and royal studies. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Natural Scientist](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3468) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 53 **Perception** +11 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +5, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +8, [Scouting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +5, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +8 **Str** +1 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +1 **Never Lost** The natural scientist can always tell true north and gains a +4 circumstance bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406). They don't take a –2 item penalty to the check if they don't have a [compass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2716).
**Trained Observer** The natural scientist is accustomed to blending into their surroundings and taking notes, giving them a +2 circumstance bonus to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391). **Items** [Explorer's Clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=2), [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12), [Writing Set](/Equipment.aspx?ID=63) --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +5 **Will** +11 **HP** 25 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** staff +7 ([two-handed d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning **Ranged** rock +7 ([thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning
Natural Sciences And Those Who Study Them There's a wide variety of things to explore and notate, and natural scientists want to document it all: **Astronomers** make detailed observations of the night sky.
**Biologists** may study the impact of civilization on wilderness areas.
**Botanists** may protect a newly discovered plant species.
**Chemists** may try to locate the source of a mysterious oil.
**Geologists** may rush to study a volcano.
**Zoologists** may try to track down a rare and elusive animal.
name: Natural Scientist

markdown:
Rules against hunting may insulate the private reserves of nobles or guard the viability of animal populations in shared forests during specific seasons. Poachers violate those laws—sometimes out of greed, sometimes out of desperation, and sometimes for sport. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Poacher](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3469) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 53 **Perception** +9 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +4, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +4, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +7 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Expert Subsistence** While using [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368), if the poacher rolls any result worse than a success, they get a success. On a success, they can provide subsistence living for themselves and four additional creatures, and on a critical success, they can take care of twice as many creatures as on a success
**Snare Crafting** The poacher knows how to craft the following [snares](/Rules.aspx?ID=3315): [alarm snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3363), [hampering snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3374), [marking snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3377), and [signaling snare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3383). The poacher can create up to four snares each day without paying for the materials, using 3 Interact actions to deploy a snare. The snare becomes inert after 24 hours. **Items** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows), [Light Mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=5), [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=3), snare toolkit (functions as [artisan's toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703)) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +10 **Will** +7 **HP** 30 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** light mace +11, **Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning **Melee** fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+4 bludgeoning **Ranged** composite shortbow +11 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing **On the Hunt** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The poacher designates one creature they're observing or tracking as their prey. The poacher gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) the prey and to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the prey. The first time the poacher hits the designated prey in a round, they deal an additional 1d4 precision damage. These effects last until the poacher uses On the Hunt again.
Penalties For Poaching Punishment for poaching on noble land can be vicious. Maiming poachers by removing fingers is a common practice. Crueler punishments include snaring poachers in their own traps and leaving them helpless, binding them in the bloody skins of their catch, and setting dogs to chase them through the woods.
name: Poacher

<title level="1" pfs="">[Chronicler](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3470)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Exploration means very little if no account of the expedition exists. Members of adventuring bands cherish chroniclers who record tales of their deeds.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<image src="/images/Monsters/Chronicler.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 54

**Perception** +12

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +10, [Scribing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +7, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +10

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +2

**Con** +1

**Int** +3

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=67) (20 bolts), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), journal, [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4), [maps](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3585), [_scroll of acid grip_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436), [_scroll of heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554), [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +8 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +10 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 45

</row>

**Live to Tell the Tale** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The chronicler would gain the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition; **Effect** The chronicler instead falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) for 1d4 hours or until they regain 1 Hit Point.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+5 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
staff +8 ([two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)),
**Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+5 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
crossbow +7 ([range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 1](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d8+3 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dagger +8 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 1d4+5 piercing

**Primal Prepared Spells** DC 20, attack +12
- **Cantrips (2nd)**
[Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Sigil](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713)
- **1st**
[Fleet Step](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [Tailwind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1711), [Vanishing Tracks](/Spells.aspx?ID=1737)
- **2nd**
[Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515), [Floating Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=1533)

**Scroll Mastery**   The chronicler can activate any scroll of a 2nd-rank spell or lower, regardless of its magical tradition.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-537" />
name: Chronicler

An untrained eye might spot a sign or two of a wild creature's passing, but only a skilled tracker can identify several such signs and discern their relationship to each other, connecting one to the next until they form a trail of prints, scat, fur, feathers, and blood that leads to the quarry's lair. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Tracker](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3471) **Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 54 **Perception** +12 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +5, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +13 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Expert Subsistence** While using [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368), if the tracker rolls any result worse than a success, they get a success. On a success, they can provide subsistence living for themselves and eight additional creatures, and on a critical success, they can take care of twice as many creatures as on a success.
**Master Tracker** The tracker can [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) while moving at full speed. **Items** [Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74) (60 arrows), [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=4) --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +11 **Will** +9 **HP** 40 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dagger +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+6 piercing **Melee** fist +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)), **Damage** 1d4+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** dagger +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+6 piercing **Ranged** composite longbow +11 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+5 piercing **On the Hunt** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The tracker designates one creature they're observing or tracking as their prey. The tracker gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) the prey and to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the prey. The first time the tracker hits the designated prey in a round, they deal an additional 1d4 precision damage. These effects last until the tracker uses On the Hunt again.
name: Tracker

<title level="1" pfs="">[Guide](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3472)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Guides bring travelers, tourists, and adventurers into the wondrous natural world, using their expertise to avoid deadly monsters and gruesome hazards.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Guide.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Guide](/NPCs.aspx?ID=3472)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=627" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=628" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 55

**Perception** +14

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1)

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +8, [Scouting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Items**
[Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (40 arrows), [Greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21), [Scale Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +8 

**Will** +11 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Guide's Warning** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The guide is about to roll a Perception or [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) check to determine their initiative; **Effect** The guide visually or audibly warns allies, granting them a +1 circumstance bonus to their initiative rolls. This bonus increases to +2 if the guide was [Scouting](/Actions.aspx?ID=2629). Depending on how the guide warns allies, this action has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
greataxe +12 ([Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)),
**Damage** 1d12+8 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
fist +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [Unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719)),
**Damage** 1d4+8 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
composite shortbow +9 ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)),
**Damage** 1d6+6 piercing

**Guiding Words** <actions string="Single Action" />  The guide points out a weakness of a creature within 30 feet. Until the start of the guide's next turn, the guide and all allies that can hear the guiding words gain a +1 circumstance bonus to attack rolls against that creature, and the guide's Strikes deal an extra 1d4 precision damage to that creature.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-537" />
name: Guide
